value.Title: Solar Stocks To Follow Now - May 12th

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-0TW3-S3VK-N4BG-00000-00Solar Stocks To Follow Now - May 12th2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisSolar Stocks To Follow Now - May 12th

First Solar, Nextpower, T1 Energy, Solaris Energy Infrastructure, and Enphase Energy are the five Solar stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Solar stocks are shares of publicly traded companies involved in the solar energy industry, such as manufacturers of solar panels, installers of solar systems, and firms that develop solar power technology or projects. For stock market investors, these stocks offer exposure to the growth of renewable energy, but they can also be volatile because their performance is often affected by government incentives, energy prices, and broader market trends. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Solar stocks within the last several days.

First Solar FSLR

First Solar, Inc., a solar technology company, provides photovoltaic PV solar energy solutions in the United States, France, Japan, Chile, and internationally. The company manufactures and sells PV solar modules with a thin film semiconductor technology that provides a lower-carbon alternative to conventional crystalline silicon PV solar modules.

Read Our Latest Research Report on FSLR

Nextpower NXT

Nextpower, formerly known as Nextracker, an energy solutions company, provides solar trackers and software solutions for utility-scale and distributed generation solar projects in the United States and internationally. The company offers tracking solutions, which includes NX Horizon, a solar tracking solution; and NX Horizon-XTR, a terrain-following tracker designed to expand the addressable market for trackers on sites with sloped, uneven, and challenging terrain.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NXT

T1 Energy TE

T1 Energy Inc. is an energy solutions provider building an integrated supply chain for solar and batteries. T1 Energy Inc. , formerly known as FREYR Battery, is based in NEW YORK.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TE

Solaris Energy Infrastructure SEI

Solaris Energy Infrastructure, Inc. is a holding company, which engages in the manufacture of patented mobile proppant management systems that unload, store, and deliver proppant to oil and natural gas well sites. Its products include Mobile Proppant and Mobile Chemical Management Systems, and Inventory Management Software.

Read Our Latest Research Report on SEI

Enphase Energy ENPH

Enphase Energy, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs, develops, manufactures, and sells home energy solutions for the solar photovoltaic industry in the United States and internationally. The company offers semiconductor-based microinverter, which converts energy at the individual solar module level and combines with its proprietary networking and software technologies to provide energy monitoring and control.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ENPH

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-1PR3-SD20-N4N2-00000-00Solar Stocks To Watch Today - May 11th2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisSolar Stocks To Watch Today - May 11th

First Solar, Nextpower, Enphase Energy, Solaris Energy Infrastructure, T1 Energy, SolarEdge Technologies, and Sunrun are the seven Solar stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Solar stocks are shares of publicly traded companies that are involved in the solar energy industry, such as solar panel manufacturers, installers, equipment suppliers, or companies that develop solar power projects. For stock market investors, the term usually refers to investments tied to the growth of solar energy demand, government incentives, and broader trends in renewable energy. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Solar stocks within the last several days.

First Solar FSLR

First Solar, Inc., a solar technology company, provides photovoltaic PV solar energy solutions in the United States, France, Japan, Chile, and internationally. The company manufactures and sells PV solar modules with a thin film semiconductor technology that provides a lower-carbon alternative to conventional crystalline silicon PV solar modules.

Read Our Latest Research Report on FSLR

Nextpower NXT

Nextpower, formerly known as Nextracker, an energy solutions company, provides solar trackers and software solutions for utility-scale and distributed generation solar projects in the United States and internationally. The company offers tracking solutions, which includes NX Horizon, a solar tracking solution; and NX Horizon-XTR, a terrain-following tracker designed to expand the addressable market for trackers on sites with sloped, uneven, and challenging terrain.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NXT

Enphase Energy ENPH

Enphase Energy, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs, develops, manufactures, and sells home energy solutions for the solar photovoltaic industry in the United States and internationally. The company offers semiconductor-based microinverter, which converts energy at the individual solar module level and combines with its proprietary networking and software technologies to provide energy monitoring and control.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ENPH

Solaris Energy Infrastructure SEI

Solaris Energy Infrastructure, Inc. is a holding company, which engages in the manufacture of patented mobile proppant management systems that unload, store, and deliver proppant to oil and natural gas well sites. Its products include Mobile Proppant and Mobile Chemical Management Systems, and Inventory Management Software.

Read Our Latest Research Report on SEI

T1 Energy TE

T1 Energy Inc. is an energy solutions provider building an integrated supply chain for solar and batteries. T1 Energy Inc. , formerly known as FREYR Battery, is based in NEW YORK.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TE

SolarEdge Technologies SEDG

SolarEdge Technologies, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs, develops, manufactures, and sells direct current DC optimized inverter systems for solar photovoltaic PV installations in the United States, Germany, the Netherlands, Italy, rest of Europe, and internationally. It operates in two segments, Solar and Energy Storage.

Read Our Latest Research Report on SEDG

Sunrun RUN

Sunrun Inc. designs, develops, installs, sells, owns, and maintains residential solar energy systems in the United States. It also sells solar energy systems and products, such as panels and racking; and solar leads generated to customers. In addition, the company offers battery storage along with solar energy systems; and sells services to commercial developers through multi-family and new homes.

Read Our Latest Research Report on RUN

May 4th, 2026 ( DailyPolitical - Delivered by Newstex )

Central Puerto ( NYSE:CEPU - Get Free Report) is expected to post its results before the market opens on Tuesday, May 12th. Analysts expect the company to announce earnings of $0.2562 per share and revenue of $241.5160 million for the quarter.

Central Puerto Stock Performance

Shares of Central Puerto stock traded up $0.14 during trading hours on Tuesday, hitting $14.43. 44,173 shares of the company's stock traded hands, compared to its average volume of 354,783. Central Puerto has a 12-month low of $7.43 and a 12-month high of $18.50. The company has a fifty day simple moving average of $15.53 and a 200-day simple moving average of $15.44. The company has a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.13, a current ratio of 1.77 and a quick ratio of 1.67. The stock has a market capitalization of $2.18 billion, a price-to-earnings ratio of 8.61 and a beta of 0.84.

Institutional Inflows and Outflows

A number of institutional investors have recently added to or reduced their stakes in CEPU. Van ECK Associates Corp bought a new position in shares of Central Puerto during the 3rd quarter valued at about $60,000. Squarepoint Ops LLC bought a new stake in shares of Central Puerto in the 3rd quarter worth approximately $138,000. Brooklyn Investment Group purchased a new position in Central Puerto during the 4th quarter valued at $174,000. Balyasny Asset Management L.P. bought a new position in Central Puerto in the 4th quarter worth $202,000. Finally, Bank of America Corp DE boosted its stake in shares of Central Puerto by 13.6% during the 2nd quarter. Bank of America Corp DE now owns 20,531 shares of the company's stock valued at $240,000 after purchasing an additional 2,460 shares in the last quarter. Institutional investors own 2.97% of the company's stock.

Analysts Set New Price Targets

CEPU has been the subject of several research analyst reports. Weiss Ratings restated a "hold (c-)" rating on shares of Central Puerto in a research note on Friday, March 27th. Wall Street Zen lowered Central Puerto from a "strong-buy" rating to a "buy" rating in a report on Saturday, March 14th. One equities research analyst has rated the stock with a Buy rating and one has issued a Hold rating to the company. According to data from MarketBeat.com, Central Puerto has a consensus rating of "Moderate Buy" and a consensus target price of $17.50.

Read Our Latest Analysis on CEPU

Central Puerto Company Profile

( Get Free Report)

Central Puerto SA (NYSE: CEPU) is Argentina's leading private power generation company, managing a diversified portfolio of thermal and renewable energy facilities across the country. Established following the privatization of the state-owned electricity company in the early 1990s, Central Puerto develops, operates and maintains a mix of combined-cycle and open-cycle gas turbine plants, as well as hydroelectric and renewable installations. The company's thermal assets provide baseload and flexible generation capacity, while its renewable portfolio includes wind farms and solar parks that support Argentina's clean energy objectives.

Headquartered in Buenos Aires, Central Puerto serves the national wholesale electricity market through long-term contracts with distribution companies and major industrial clients.

Link to the original story.

Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Hanz Christensen

May 11th, 2026 ( Watchlist News - Delivered by Newstex )

First Solar, Nextpower, Enphase Energy, Solaris Energy Infrastructure, T1 Energy, SolarEdge Technologies, and Sunrun are the seven Solar stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Solar stocks are shares of publicly traded companies that are involved in the solar energy industry, such as solar panel manufacturers, installers, equipment suppliers, or companies that develop solar power projects. For stock market investors, the term usually refers to investments tied to the growth of solar energy demand, government incentives, and broader trends in renewable energy. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Solar stocks within the last several days.

First Solar (FSLR)

First Solar, Inc., a solar technology company, provides photovoltaic (PV) solar energy solutions in the United States, France, Japan, Chile, and internationally. The company manufactures and sells PV solar modules with a thin film semiconductor technology that provides a lower-carbon alternative to conventional crystalline silicon PV solar modules.

Read Our Latest Research Report on FSLR

Nextpower (NXT)

Nextpower, formerly known as Nextracker, an energy solutions company, provides solar trackers and software solutions for utility-scale and distributed generation solar projects in the United States and internationally. The company offers tracking solutions, which includes NX Horizon, a solar tracking solution; and NX Horizon-XTR, a terrain-following tracker designed to expand the addressable market for trackers on sites with sloped, uneven, and challenging terrain.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NXT

Enphase Energy (ENPH)

Enphase Energy, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs, develops, manufactures, and sells home energy solutions for the solar photovoltaic industry in the United States and internationally. The company offers semiconductor-based microinverter, which converts energy at the individual solar module level and combines with its proprietary networking and software technologies to provide energy monitoring and control.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ENPH

Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI)

Solaris Energy Infrastructure, Inc. is a holding company, which engages in the manufacture of patented mobile proppant management systems that unload, store, and deliver proppant to oil and natural gas well sites. Its products include Mobile Proppant and Mobile Chemical Management Systems, and Inventory Management Software.

Read Our Latest Research Report on SEI

T1 Energy (TE)

T1 Energy Inc. is an energy solutions provider building an integrated supply chain for solar and batteries. T1 Energy Inc. , formerly known as FREYR Battery, is based in NEW YORK.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TE

SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG)

SolarEdge Technologies, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs, develops, manufactures, and sells direct current (DC) optimized inverter systems for solar photovoltaic (PV) installations in the United States, Germany, the Netherlands, Italy, rest of Europe, and internationally. It operates in two segments, Solar and Energy Storage.

Read Our Latest Research Report on SEDG

Sunrun (RUN)

Sunrun Inc. designs, develops, installs, sells, owns, and maintains residential solar energy systems in the United States. It also sells solar energy systems and products, such as panels and racking; and solar leads generated to customers. In addition, the company offers battery storage along with solar energy systems; and sells services to commercial developers through multi-family and new homes.

Read Our Latest Research Report on RUN

Link to the original story.

May 10th, 2026 ( DailyPolitical - Delivered by Newstex )

First Solar, Nextpower, Enphase Energy, Solaris Energy Infrastructure, T1 Energy, SolarEdge Technologies, and Sunrun are the seven Solar stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Solar stocks are shares of publicly traded companies that are involved in the solar energy industry, such as solar panel manufacturers, installers, equipment suppliers, or companies that develop solar power projects. For stock market investors, the term usually refers to investments tied to the growth of solar energy demand, government incentives, and broader trends in renewable energy. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Solar stocks within the last several days.

First Solar (FSLR)

First Solar, Inc., a solar technology company, provides photovoltaic (PV) solar energy solutions in the United States, France, Japan, Chile, and internationally. The company manufactures and sells PV solar modules with a thin film semiconductor technology that provides a lower-carbon alternative to conventional crystalline silicon PV solar modules.

Read Our Latest Research Report on FSLR

Nextpower (NXT)

Nextpower, formerly known as Nextracker, an energy solutions company, provides solar trackers and software solutions for utility-scale and distributed generation solar projects in the United States and internationally. The company offers tracking solutions, which includes NX Horizon, a solar tracking solution; and NX Horizon-XTR, a terrain-following tracker designed to expand the addressable market for trackers on sites with sloped, uneven, and challenging terrain.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NXT

Enphase Energy (ENPH)

Enphase Energy, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs, develops, manufactures, and sells home energy solutions for the solar photovoltaic industry in the United States and internationally. The company offers semiconductor-based microinverter, which converts energy at the individual solar module level and combines with its proprietary networking and software technologies to provide energy monitoring and control.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ENPH

Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI)

Solaris Energy Infrastructure, Inc. is a holding company, which engages in the manufacture of patented mobile proppant management systems that unload, store, and deliver proppant to oil and natural gas well sites. Its products include Mobile Proppant and Mobile Chemical Management Systems, and Inventory Management Software.

Read Our Latest Research Report on SEI

T1 Energy (TE)

T1 Energy Inc. is an energy solutions provider building an integrated supply chain for solar and batteries. T1 Energy Inc. , formerly known as FREYR Battery, is based in NEW YORK.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TE

SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG)

SolarEdge Technologies, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs, develops, manufactures, and sells direct current (DC) optimized inverter systems for solar photovoltaic (PV) installations in the United States, Germany, the Netherlands, Italy, rest of Europe, and internationally. It operates in two segments, Solar and Energy Storage.

Read Our Latest Research Report on SEDG

Sunrun (RUN)

Sunrun Inc. designs, develops, installs, sells, owns, and maintains residential solar energy systems in the United States. It also sells solar energy systems and products, such as panels and racking; and solar leads generated to customers. In addition, the company offers battery storage along with solar energy systems; and sells services to commercial developers through multi-family and new homes.

Read Our Latest Research Report on RUN

Link to the original story.

urn:contentItem:6JHD-4CF3-RYDK-80D6-00000-00How Baker Hughes Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisHow Baker Hughes Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

During President Trump's second term, corporate investment in the United States has increased, with major companies in technology, manufacturing, and healthcare allocating billions to domestic expansion. The semiconductor industry has committed over $500 billion in private-sector funding, indicating a notable reshoring trend. This investment activity is backed by enhanced tax incentives from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, including 100% bonus depreciation and 35% manufacturing credits, and tariff policies aimed at supporting domestic production.

The reshoring trend affects multiple high-value sectors. Semiconductor manufacturing investments of over $500 billion across U.S. facilities are expected to create more than 150,000 new jobs, while pharmaceutical companies have allocated over $150 billion for U.S. manufacturing investments. Foreign direct investment was recorded at $102 billion in Q2 2025, representing a 137% increase from Q1. This comprehensive restructuring is influenced by both policy incentives and supply chain resilience priorities.

This report examines investment commitments from companies across the technology, manufacturing, healthcare, and energy sectors.

Baker Hughes Company (NASDAQ: BKR)

- Company Profile: Baker Hughes Company (NASDAQ: BKR) is a U.S.-based energy technology firm that supplies oilfield services and equipment, with about 55,000 employees globally.

- Announced Investment: $1.5 billion is allocated for 2025-2026 to advance carbon capture and hydrogen technology, and expand U.S. manufacturing for energy transition equipment.

- Recipient: Baker Hughes U.S. Energy Transition Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Energy Technology, Manufacturing

Cheniere Energy, Inc. (NYSE: LNG)

- Company Profile: Cheniere Energy, Inc. (NYSE: LNG) is a U.S.-based LNG firm that runs liquefaction and export facilities, employing approximately 1,800 people globally.

- Announced Investment: A $4 billion investment for 2025-2026 is aimed at expanding the Sabine Pass and Corpus Christi LNG facilities to increase export capacity.

- Recipient: Cheniere Energy U.S. LNG Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Energy, LNG Export

Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED)

- Company Profile: Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED) is a U.S.-based utility that provides electric, gas, and steam services, with a global employee count of approximately 15,000.

- Announced Investment: $2.8 billion planned for 2025-2026 to upgrade the New York City electric grid, improve resilience to weather, and increase renewable energy connections.

- Recipient: Consolidated Edison U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

Crown Castle International (NYSE: CCI)

- Company Profile: Crown Castle International (NYSE: CCI) is a U.S.-based telecommunications infrastructure firm that manages towers and fiber networks, with a team of approximately 7,000.

- Announced Investment: $3.5 billion for 2025-2026 to build out fiber-to-the-premises networks and 5G infrastructure throughout the U.S.

- Recipient: Crown Castle U.S. Telecom Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Telecommunications, Infrastructure.

Deere and Company

- Company Profile: Deere and Company (NYSE: DE) is a firm specializing in agricultural machinery and equipment manufacturing, employing approximately 82,000 people worldwide.

- Announced Investment: $2 billion in 2025 to enlarge Illinois and Iowa manufacturing facilities for the production of advanced machinery.

- Recipient: Deere U.S. Manufacturing Plants.

- Industries Impacted: Manufacturing, Agriculture.

Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: FANG)

- Company Profile: Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: FANG) is a U.S.-based oil and gas firm concentrated on Permian Basin operations, employing approximately 900 people globally.

- Announced Investment: $3.8 billion in a 2025 capital program for drilling and completion activities in the Permian Basin, increasing production capacity.

- Recipient: Diamondback Energy U.S. Permian Operations.

- Industries Impacted: Energy, Oil and Gas.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE: DTE)

- Company Profile: DTE Energy Company (NYSE: DTE) is a U.S.-based utility firm that supplies electric and gas services, employing approximately 10,000 people worldwide.

- Announced Investment: $2.3 billion for 2025-2026 to increase renewable energy capacity, update the Michigan electric grid, and develop natural gas infrastructure.

- Recipient: DTE Energy U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ: EXC)

- Company Profile: Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ: EXC) is a U.S.-based utility company that operates electric and gas utilities, with a global workforce of approximately 33,000.

- Announced Investment: $4.5 billion for 2025-2026 to modernize electric grid infrastructure, improve reliability, and increase clean energy integration in its service territories.

- Recipient: Exelon U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

FirstEnergy Corp. (NYSE: FE)

- Company Profile: FirstEnergy Corp. is an energy infrastructure operator with a workforce of approximately 15,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A multi-year investment of $15 billion is designated for infrastructure upgrades and grid modernization projects.

- Recipient: FirstEnergy U.S. Energy Grid

- Industries Impacted: Energy

First Solar Inc. (NASDAQ: FSLR)

- Company Profile: First Solar Inc., a U.S.-headquartered manufacturer of solar panels, maintains production facilities worldwide and employs about 6,400 people.

- Announced Investment: An allocation of $1.1 billion in 2025 will fund the construction of a new solar panel manufacturing plant in Ohio. A separate $1.1 billion, 3.5 GW facility in Louisiana is scheduled to be operational in late 2025.

- Recipient: First Solar U.S. Solar Panel Plants

- Industries Impacted: Renewable Energy, Manufacturing

Occidental Petroleum (NYSE: OXY)

- Company Profile: A U.S. energy company with a workforce of approximately 12,000 employees.

- Announced Investment: A $1.8 billion investment is planned for July 2025 for Texas enhanced oil recovery and direct air capture (DAC) plants.

- Recipient: U.S. energy sites

- Industries Impacted: Energy

ONEOK Inc. (NYSE: OKE)

- Company Profile: ONEOK, Inc., a U.S.-based energy company, manages extensive natural gas pipeline networks and employs approximately 3,000 individuals.

- Announced Investment: In August 2025, ONEOK confirmed its involvement in the Eiger Express Pipeline, a $1.2 billion, An error occurred during generation.

- Industries Impacted: Energy

Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE: PEG)

- Company Profile: Public Service Enterprise Group is a U.S.-based energy firm managing utilities and power generation, employing approximately 13,000 people globally.

- Announced Investment: A sum of $3.2 billion is designated for 2025-2026 to build offshore wind infrastructure, update transmission systems, and increase clean energy generation in New Jersey.

- Recipient: Public Service Enterprise Group U.S. Energy Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Renewable Energy

Rollins Inc. (NYSE: ROL)

- Company Profile: Rollins Inc. (NYSE: ROL) is a U.S.-headquartered pest control and termite services firm, with a global workforce of around 18,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A $1 billion capital injection in 2025 is allocated for fleet modernization and facility expansions.

- Recipient: Rollins U.S. Operations Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Services, Facilities

Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT)

- Company Profile: Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT), a prominent U.S.-headquartered retailer, manages stores and distribution networks with a workforce of around 440,000 employees.

- Announced Investment: A $1 billion development plan for 2025 is intended to improve distribution centers in Minnesota to accommodate e-commerce expansion.

- Recipient: Target U.S. Retail Operations

- Industries Impacted: Retail, Logistics

Teradyne Inc. (NASDAQ: TER)

- Company Profile: Teradyne Inc. (NASDAQ: TER) is a U.S.-headquartered semiconductor test equipment producer, with a global workforce of around 5,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A $1.4 billion capital injection in 2025 is set for the expansion of manufacturing and R&D facilities.

- Recipient: Teradyne U.S. Manufacturing Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Semiconductors, Manufacturing

Xcel Energy Inc. (NASDAQ: XEL)

- Company Profile: Xcel Energy Inc. is a U.S.-based utility company that delivers electricity and natural gas and has a global workforce of approximately 11,000.

- Announced Investment: A $2.5 billion expenditure in 2025-2026 will support renewable energy generation, transmission upgrades, and distribution modernization across its service area.

- Recipient: Xcel Energy U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Renewable Energy.

Zoom Video Communications Inc. (NASDAQ: ZM)

- Company Profile: Zoom Video Communications Inc. is a U.S.-based video conferencing and communications platform company with a global staff of approximately 9,000.

- Announced Investment: A $1.5 billion fund in 2025 is planned for the expansion of data center infrastructure and AI capabilities.

- Recipient: Zoom U.S. Technology Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Technology, Cloud Computing.

Baker Hughes Company share has been a two bagger in the past year. A year ago the Baker Hughes Company share price was $36.36. $1,000 would have bought 27.5 Baker Hughes Company shares on that day. Those 27.5 Shares would be worth $1,898 at today's share price of $69.01. Dividends reinvested are worth $12. PV$1000= $1,910.

Activities

Baker Hughes Company is a global leader in energy technology, headquartered in the United States and listed on the Nasdaq exchange. The company specializes in providing innovative solutions for the oil and gas sector, including oilfield services, subsea production systems, turbomachinery, and process technologies that support exploration, drilling, and production operations worldwide. Its core business also extends to industrial applications and digital services, such as advanced data analytics and artificial intelligence tools that enhance operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are digital solutions, which leverage IoT and predictive analytics to optimize asset performance, and new energy initiatives focused on hydrogen production, carbon capture, and energy storage. These areas are fueling expansion by addressing the industry's shift toward sustainability and efficiency, solidifying Baker Hughes' role as a key innovator in the evolving energy landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Energy company by market capitalisation.

Baker Hughes Company, NASDAQ:BKR, has cemented its place as a leader in the energy sector with advanced solutions like the Baker Hughes pipeline turbine and pipeline turbine order. The company's pipeline turbines for LNG and gas turbines for pipelines are critical in meeting the demands of modern energy infrastructure. Their pipeline compressor driver turbines and compressor turbines for LNG projects demonstrate Baker Hughes' commitment to delivering cutting-edge turbomachinery for LNG. Offering industrial gas turbines, including aeroderivative gas turbines and heavy-duty gas turbines, Baker Hughes ensures efficient energy solutions. Their pipeline compressor packages and compressor station turbines support the efficient operation of pipeline booster station turbines. Utilizing natural gas pipeline turbines, they provide robust turbine drivers for compressor stations. As part of Baker Hughes turbomachinery offerings, the company delivers comprehensive Baker Hughes compressor solutions. Notable projects include Argentina LNG project turbines and turnkey compressor packages. Their engineered compressor drivers and gas turbine maintenance services are complemented by gas turbine retrofit solutions, rotating equipment for pipelines, and turbine spare parts for compressors. Baker Hughes excels with dual-fuel gas turbines, liquefied natural gas turbomachinery, pipeline mechanical drive turbines, and compressor driver spare parts, all while providing turboexpanders for LNG and gas turbine emission control systems.

urn:contentItem:6JHD-9093-SDNH-R2GT-00000-00How Xcel Energy Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:00ZLexisNexisHow Xcel Energy Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

During President Trump's second term, corporate investment in the United States has increased, with major companies in technology, manufacturing, and healthcare allocating billions to domestic expansion. The semiconductor industry has committed over $500 billion in private-sector funding, indicating a notable reshoring trend. This investment activity is backed by enhanced tax incentives from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, including 100% bonus depreciation and 35% manufacturing credits, and tariff policies aimed at supporting domestic production.

The reshoring trend affects multiple high-value sectors. Semiconductor manufacturing investments of over $500 billion across U.S. facilities are expected to create more than 150,000 new jobs, while pharmaceutical companies have allocated over $150 billion for U.S. manufacturing investments. Foreign direct investment was recorded at $102 billion in Q2 2025, representing a 137% increase from Q1. This comprehensive restructuring is influenced by both policy incentives and supply chain resilience priorities.

This report examines investment commitments from companies across the technology, manufacturing, healthcare, and energy sectors.

Xcel Energy Inc. (NASDAQ: XEL)

- Company Profile: Xcel Energy Inc. is a U.S.-based utility company that delivers electricity and natural gas and has a global workforce of approximately 11,000.

- Announced Investment: A $2.5 billion expenditure in 2025-2026 will support renewable energy generation, transmission upgrades, and distribution modernization across its service area.

- Recipient: Xcel Energy U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Renewable Energy.

Baker Hughes Company (NASDAQ: BKR)

- Company Profile: Baker Hughes Company (NASDAQ: BKR) is a U.S.-based energy technology firm that supplies oilfield services and equipment, with about 55,000 employees globally.

- Announced Investment: $1.5 billion is allocated for 2025-2026 to advance carbon capture and hydrogen technology, and expand U.S. manufacturing for energy transition equipment.

- Recipient: Baker Hughes U.S. Energy Transition Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Energy Technology, Manufacturing

Cheniere Energy, Inc. (NYSE: LNG)

- Company Profile: Cheniere Energy, Inc. (NYSE: LNG) is a U.S.-based LNG firm that runs liquefaction and export facilities, employing approximately 1,800 people globally.

- Announced Investment: A $4 billion investment for 2025-2026 is aimed at expanding the Sabine Pass and Corpus Christi LNG facilities to increase export capacity.

- Recipient: Cheniere Energy U.S. LNG Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Energy, LNG Export

Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED)

- Company Profile: Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED) is a U.S.-based utility that provides electric, gas, and steam services, with a global employee count of approximately 15,000.

- Announced Investment: $2.8 billion planned for 2025-2026 to upgrade the New York City electric grid, improve resilience to weather, and increase renewable energy connections.

- Recipient: Consolidated Edison U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

Crown Castle International (NYSE: CCI)

- Company Profile: Crown Castle International (NYSE: CCI) is a U.S.-based telecommunications infrastructure firm that manages towers and fiber networks, with a team of approximately 7,000.

- Announced Investment: $3.5 billion for 2025-2026 to build out fiber-to-the-premises networks and 5G infrastructure throughout the U.S.

- Recipient: Crown Castle U.S. Telecom Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Telecommunications, Infrastructure.

Deere and Company

- Company Profile: Deere and Company (NYSE: DE) is a firm specializing in agricultural machinery and equipment manufacturing, employing approximately 82,000 people worldwide.

- Announced Investment: $2 billion in 2025 to enlarge Illinois and Iowa manufacturing facilities for the production of advanced machinery.

- Recipient: Deere U.S. Manufacturing Plants.

- Industries Impacted: Manufacturing, Agriculture.

Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: FANG)

- Company Profile: Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: FANG) is a U.S.-based oil and gas firm concentrated on Permian Basin operations, employing approximately 900 people globally.

- Announced Investment: $3.8 billion in a 2025 capital program for drilling and completion activities in the Permian Basin, increasing production capacity.

- Recipient: Diamondback Energy U.S. Permian Operations.

- Industries Impacted: Energy, Oil and Gas.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE: DTE)

- Company Profile: DTE Energy Company (NYSE: DTE) is a U.S.-based utility firm that supplies electric and gas services, employing approximately 10,000 people worldwide.

- Announced Investment: $2.3 billion for 2025-2026 to increase renewable energy capacity, update the Michigan electric grid, and develop natural gas infrastructure.

- Recipient: DTE Energy U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ: EXC)

- Company Profile: Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ: EXC) is a U.S.-based utility company that operates electric and gas utilities, with a global workforce of approximately 33,000.

- Announced Investment: $4.5 billion for 2025-2026 to modernize electric grid infrastructure, improve reliability, and increase clean energy integration in its service territories.

- Recipient: Exelon U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

FirstEnergy Corp. (NYSE: FE)

- Company Profile: FirstEnergy Corp. is an energy infrastructure operator with a workforce of approximately 15,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A multi-year investment of $15 billion is designated for infrastructure upgrades and grid modernization projects.

- Recipient: FirstEnergy U.S. Energy Grid

- Industries Impacted: Energy

First Solar Inc. (NASDAQ: FSLR)

- Company Profile: First Solar Inc., a U.S.-headquartered manufacturer of solar panels, maintains production facilities worldwide and employs about 6,400 people.

- Announced Investment: An allocation of $1.1 billion in 2025 will fund the construction of a new solar panel manufacturing plant in Ohio. A separate $1.1 billion, 3.5 GW facility in Louisiana is scheduled to be operational in late 2025.

- Recipient: First Solar U.S. Solar Panel Plants

- Industries Impacted: Renewable Energy, Manufacturing

Occidental Petroleum (NYSE: OXY)

- Company Profile: A U.S. energy company with a workforce of approximately 12,000 employees.

- Announced Investment: A $1.8 billion investment is planned for July 2025 for Texas enhanced oil recovery and direct air capture (DAC) plants.

- Recipient: U.S. energy sites

- Industries Impacted: Energy

ONEOK Inc. (NYSE: OKE)

- Company Profile: ONEOK, Inc., a U.S.-based energy company, manages extensive natural gas pipeline networks and employs approximately 3,000 individuals.

- Announced Investment: In August 2025, ONEOK confirmed its involvement in the Eiger Express Pipeline, a $1.2 billion, An error occurred during generation.

- Industries Impacted: Energy

- Company Profile: Public Service Enterprise Group is a U.S.-based energy firm managing utilities and power generation, employing approximately 13,000 people globally.

- Announced Investment: A sum of $3.2 billion is designated for 2025-2026 to build offshore wind infrastructure, update transmission systems, and increase clean energy generation in New Jersey.

- Recipient: Public Service Enterprise Group U.S. Energy Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Renewable Energy

Rollins Inc. (NYSE: ROL)

- Company Profile: Rollins Inc. (NYSE: ROL) is a U.S.-headquartered pest control and termite services firm, with a global workforce of around 18,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A $1 billion capital injection in 2025 is allocated for fleet modernization and facility expansions.

- Recipient: Rollins U.S. Operations Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Services, Facilities

Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT)

- Company Profile: Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT), a prominent U.S.-headquartered retailer, manages stores and distribution networks with a workforce of around 440,000 employees.

- Announced Investment: A $1 billion development plan for 2025 is intended to improve distribution centers in Minnesota to accommodate e-commerce expansion.

- Recipient: Target U.S. Retail Operations

- Industries Impacted: Retail, Logistics

Teradyne Inc. (NASDAQ: TER)

- Company Profile: Teradyne Inc. (NASDAQ: TER) is a U.S.-headquartered semiconductor test equipment producer, with a global workforce of around 5,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A $1.4 billion capital injection in 2025 is set for the expansion of manufacturing and R&D facilities.

- Recipient: Teradyne U.S. Manufacturing Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Semiconductors, Manufacturing

Zoom Video Communications Inc. (NASDAQ: ZM)

- Company Profile: Zoom Video Communications Inc. is a U.S.-based video conferencing and communications platform company with a global staff of approximately 9,000.

- Announced Investment: A $1.5 billion fund in 2025 is planned for the expansion of data center infrastructure and AI capabilities.

- Recipient: Zoom U.S. Technology Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Technology, Cloud Computing.

A year ago the Xcel Energy Inc. share price was $68.72. $1,000 would have bought 14.6 Xcel Energy Inc. shares on that day. Those 14.6 Shares would be worth $1,185 at today's share price of $81.45. Dividends reinvested are worth $9. PV$1000= $1,195.

Activities

Xcel Energy Inc., headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota, is a leading U.S.-based utility holding company that operates primarily in the electric and natural gas sectors. As a major provider serving approximately 3.7 million electric customers and 2.1 million natural gas customers across eight states, including Colorado, Minnesota, and Wisconsin, the company's core activities encompass the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas. Xcel Energy focuses on reliable energy delivery through a diversified portfolio that includes coal, nuclear, and increasingly, renewable sources. The fastest-growing segments of its business are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power, driven by strategic investments in clean energy projects and a commitment to reducing carbon emissions, with goals to achieve 100% carbon-free electricity by 2050. This shift not only supports regulatory demands and customer preferences for sustainable options but also positions the company for long-term growth in a transitioning energy market. Xcel Energy maintains a strong emphasis on operational efficiency, safety, and innovation to ensure stable returns for shareholders. It is the NASDAQ's 3rd largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Xcel Energy Inc., NASDAQ:XEL, is at the forefront of the renewable energy transition, making significant strides in solar energy expansion and utility-scale battery storage projects. Their 200 MW battery storage initiative and the Minnesota VPP expansion approval demonstrate a commitment to distributed energy resources (DER) integration and electric grid modernization. The company's utility-owned VPP program aims to enhance energy reliability services and optimize energy cost management, particularly through virtual power plant (VPP) technologies. As part of their clean energy strategy, Xcel Energy clean energy strategy includes a $130 million nuclear tax credit refund to Upper Midwest customers, showcasing their dedication to clean energy leadership for investors. Recent Capacity*Connect program and Denver airport power outage investigation highlight their focus on outage response and mitigation. With XEL stock clean energy push, Xcel Energy is committed to power infrastructure for AI scaling and backup power solutions. This aligns with efforts like backup battery rebates and the winter moratorium on disconnections for vulnerable customers. Investor news Xcel Energy consistently reflects its position as a leader in renewable and reliable energy solutions.

value.Title: Enphase Energy May Evaluate Supply Chain Adjustments Following Advanced Energy Industries Power Authority Litigation *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-9093-SDNH-R2HS-00000-00Enphase Energy May Evaluate Supply Chain Adjustments Following Advanced Energy Industries Power Authority Litigation *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisEnphase Energy May Evaluate Supply Chain Adjustments Following Advanced Energy Industries Power Authority Litigation *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

What Stakeholders Need To Know:

This matter illustrates the continued examination of product performance representations in publicly funded renewable energy projects. The litigation indicates potential liabilities when equipment supplied for state-supported installations does not meet expected standards, leading to a closer review of supply chain disclosures and warranty practices across the solar equipment sector. Outcomes may influence how manufacturers manage contractual obligations tied to government funding, potentially elevating compliance costs and altering procurement standards for large-scale solar deployments. Broader industry effects could include increased investor focus on quality assurance protocols and risk disclosures in emerging energy infrastructure. The case demonstrates the intersection of state procurement rules and private relator actions, which may promote more rigorous internal testing and documentation processes sector-wide.

Background of the Case

The Otto Candies litigation originated in 2016 following the 2014 dissolution of Oceanografia S.A. de C.V., a Mexican oil-services contractor. Reports indicate that Oceanografia presented thousands of falsified Pemex receivables to Citigroup's subsidiary, Grupo Financiero Banamex, which advanced approximately $585 million against those invoices under a vendor-finance program. When Mexican officials suspended Oceanografia in February 2014, Banamex recognized a loss of $400 million. Institutional investors, including hedge funds, insurance firms, and pension plans that had acquired short-term Banamex-issued certificates, incurred principal losses estimated at $300 million. Claimants assert that Citigroup Inc. and senior executives based in New York knowingly participated in or recklessly disregarded a multi-year enterprise, allegedly violating civil RICO statutes and the Securities Exchange Act. While the case faced multiple dismissals based on statute-of-limitations and extraterritoriality, the 11th Circuit reversed these decisions in September 2024, ruling that sufficient domestic activity-such as New York approvals, U.S. dollar wire transfers, and regulatory filings-occurred to apply RICO domestically. Citigroup filed a petition for review in November 2024, which remains pending throughout 2025.

Competitive Impacts on Enphase Energy

Enphase Energy's microinverter platform continues to hold a premium position in residential and small commercial segments. Industry commentary notes increased customer emphasis on long-term reliability data when evaluating bids that include public funding components, creating modest differentiation opportunities without material disruption to existing revenue streams.

Possible Opportunities for Enphase Energy

Positioning microinverter systems as lower-risk alternatives in quality-focused public procurements.

Expanding pilot programs with municipalities seeking granular performance monitoring.

Enhancing marketing materials to highlight independent certification credentials.

Developing targeted financing packages for school and municipal solar initiatives.

Why This Case Is Challenging for Enphase Energy

Sector-wide elevation of warranty and performance disclosure standards may compress margins if customers demand more extensive guarantees. Incremental costs for additional testing protocols could accumulate across product lines. Competitive bidding processes may incorporate new evaluation criteria tied to litigation history, indirectly affecting win rates. Analyst coverage could broaden to include comparative quality risk assessments across the peer group.

Key Challenges for Enphase Energy

Rising costs associated with expanded third-party validation programs.

Potential customer requests for supplemental performance bonds in public bids.

Increased frequency of due-diligence inquiries regarding historical product claims.

Alignment of global warranty policies with evolving U.S. public-contract norms.

Competitor's Strategic Responses

Rollout of enhanced customer-facing reliability dashboards.

Collaboration with independent testing organizations for public-sector certifications.

Refinement of sales collateral to address procurement risk factors.

Internal audit expansion covering all government-adjacent product lines.

Key Measures

Enphase Energy coordinates these initiatives through a dedicated compliance task force that reports quarterly to executive leadership, ensuring rapid implementation across sales and engineering teams.

Possible Losses and Unexpected Advantages

Potential Advantages

Market share increase in quality-sensitive public projects, translating into additional revenue estimates of up to $35 million.

Improved customer retention through demonstrated transparency.

Faster pathway to preferred-vendor status in select states.

Opportunity to showcase technology advantages in regulated environments.

Potential Losses

Incremental compliance expenditures estimated at up to $12 million.

Margin compression from extended warranty offerings.

Temporary sales cycle extensions during heightened due diligence.

Elevated insurance costs for product liability coverage.

Final Thoughts

Enphase Energy encounters limited direct exposure yet can leverage the industry focus on quality to reinforce its premium positioning. Strategic emphasis on verifiable performance data positions the company to capture incremental share in public-sector solar while sustaining disciplined cost management.

Tags: The Power Authority of the State of New York, ex rel. Solar Liberty Energy Systems, Inc. v. Advanced Energy Industries, Inc.

Activities

Advanced Energy Industries Inc., a Nasdaq-listed company, specializes in designing, manufacturing, and supplying precision power conversion, measurement, and control solutions for high-tech industries. Its core business activities focus on enabling advanced manufacturing processes, including semiconductor fabrication, thin film deposition for solar panels and displays, and power management for data centers and industrial applications. The company leverages its expertise in power electronics to support energy-efficient technologies, serving sectors like telecommunications, life sciences, and automotive. Among its fastest-growing segments are data center infrastructure and renewable energy solutions, fueled by rising demand for AI-driven computing and sustainable power systems. This strategic emphasis on innovation and efficiency positions Advanced Energy as a key player in addressing global challenges in energy consumption and technological advancement. It is the NASDAQ's 3rd largest Semi equipment wafer company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JHD-4CF3-RYDK-80D7-00000-00How Zoom Video Communications Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisHow Zoom Video Communications Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

During President Trump's second term, corporate investment in the United States has increased, with major companies in technology, manufacturing, and healthcare allocating billions to domestic expansion. The semiconductor industry has committed over $500 billion in private-sector funding, indicating a notable reshoring trend. This investment activity is backed by enhanced tax incentives from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, including 100% bonus depreciation and 35% manufacturing credits, and tariff policies aimed at supporting domestic production.

The reshoring trend affects multiple high-value sectors. Semiconductor manufacturing investments of over $500 billion across U.S. facilities are expected to create more than 150,000 new jobs, while pharmaceutical companies have allocated over $150 billion for U.S. manufacturing investments. Foreign direct investment was recorded at $102 billion in Q2 2025, representing a 137% increase from Q1. This comprehensive restructuring is influenced by both policy incentives and supply chain resilience priorities.

This report examines investment commitments from companies across the technology, manufacturing, healthcare, and energy sectors.

Zoom Video Communications Inc. (NASDAQ: ZM)

- Company Profile: Zoom Video Communications Inc. is a U.S.-based video conferencing and communications platform company with a global staff of approximately 9,000.

- Announced Investment: A $1.5 billion fund in 2025 is planned for the expansion of data center infrastructure and AI capabilities.

- Recipient: Zoom U.S. Technology Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Technology, Cloud Computing.

Baker Hughes Company (NASDAQ: BKR)

- Company Profile: Baker Hughes Company (NASDAQ: BKR) is a U.S.-based energy technology firm that supplies oilfield services and equipment, with about 55,000 employees globally.

- Announced Investment: $1.5 billion is allocated for 2025-2026 to advance carbon capture and hydrogen technology, and expand U.S. manufacturing for energy transition equipment.

- Recipient: Baker Hughes U.S. Energy Transition Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Energy Technology, Manufacturing

Cheniere Energy, Inc. (NYSE: LNG)

- Company Profile: Cheniere Energy, Inc. (NYSE: LNG) is a U.S.-based LNG firm that runs liquefaction and export facilities, employing approximately 1,800 people globally.

- Recipient: Cheniere Energy U.S. LNG Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Energy, LNG Export

Activities

Zoom Video Communications Inc., listed on the Nasdaq, is a leading provider of cloud-based communication and collaboration platforms that enable seamless virtual interactions for businesses, education, and individuals worldwide. At its core, the company offers a suite of tools including video conferencing, webinars, chat, phone systems, and workspace solutions, all designed to enhance remote and hybrid work environments. Zoom's platform prioritizes user-friendly interfaces, high-quality video and audio, and robust security features, making it a go-to choice for enterprises seeking efficient digital communication. Among its fastest-growing segments are AI-powered enhancements, such as automated meeting summaries and virtual assistants, which are driving adoption in contact centers and customer engagement; enterprise-grade solutions like Zoom Phone and Zoom Rooms, catering to large organizations; and international expansion into emerging markets for online events and education. This strategic focus has solidified Zoom's position as an innovator in unified communications, supporting productivity and connectivity in a post-pandemic world. It is the NASDAQ's largest Telephone communications company by market capitalisation.

Zoom AI imposter detection is revolutionizing virtual communications by enhancing AI imposter detection in meetings. This cutting-edge technology, such as Zoom meeting deepfake detection and Zoom real-time voice cloning detection, ensures robust Zoom identity verification and Zoom meeting impersonation protection. With advanced Zoom security features, Zoom has partnered with World (Sam Altman) through the Zoom + World AI partnership to continually advance AI moderation in Zoom Meetings. Zoom Video, encompassing Zoom Rooms, Zoom Phone, Zoom Webinar, and Zoom Chat, delivers a comprehensive communication suite available on the Zoom Marketplace. The integration of Zoom Apps and the versatile Zoom AI Companion augments user experience. Notably, Zoom's anti-deepfake technology, Zoom authentication, and Zoom end-to-end encryption uphold Zoom compliance, including Zoom HIPAA compliance. Ideal for Zoom enterprise video conferencing, the platform's capabilities are bolstered by the Zoom SDK, Zoom Live Transcription, Zoom closed captioning, and Zoom virtual backgrounds, facilitating seamless Zoom remote collaboration.

value.Title: April 28, 2026: Rockefeller Foundation Announces Africa Big Bets Fellows Amid Nairobi Convening

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-DBX3-RXH7-23W4-00000-00April 28, 2026: Rockefeller Foundation Announces Africa Big Bets Fellows Amid Nairobi Convening2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Rockefeller Foundation Announces Africa Big Bets Fellows Amid Nairobi Convening

The Rockefeller Foundation has introduced the Africa Big Bets Fellows at AfricaXchange in Nairobi, selecting ten fellows from Ghana, Kenya, Malawi, Nigeria, South Africa, and Tanzania to lead community-led solutions.

The five-month fellowship focuses on energy access, food security, healthcare, financial inclusion, climate resilience, and migration.

On 28th of April, 2026, the date marks the 60th anniversary of the Africa Regional Office and signals the Foundation's ongoing commitment to Africa.

Examples include mobile healthcare in rural Ghana, waste-to-resource systems, regenerative agriculture in Kenya, AI-powered farming in Malawi, and wider access to financial services in Nigeria. The effort is part of AfricaXchange, which aims to connect funders with practitioners and policymakers to scale locally led development.

INDEX

SECTION 1 ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION PROFILE

SECTION 2 ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

1.2 SUMMARY

value.Title: April 28, 2026: First Cohort of Africa Big Bets Fellows Target Energy, Health and Climate Goals

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHS-DX73-RTB8-N0F1-00000-00April 28, 2026: First Cohort of Africa Big Bets Fellows Target Energy, Health and Climate Goals2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: First Cohort of Africa Big Bets Fellows Target Energy, Health and Climate Goals

The Rockefeller Foundation has launched the Africa Big Bets Fellows at AfricaXchange in Nairobi, presenting a cohort of ten changemakers from six countries. These fellows will drive community-led projects across critical zones such as energy access, food security, healthcare, financial inclusion, climate resilience, and migration, over a five-month program.

The program's timing and scale reflect Africa's ongoing development challenges and opportunities. The launch occurs on 28th of April, 2026, marking the 60th anniversary of the Africa Regional Office.

Projects include mobile healthcare in rural Ghana, waste-to-resource systems for cleaner cities, regenerative agriculture tied to school feeding in Kenya, AI-powered farming in Malawi, and expanded financial services in Nigeria. The initiative sits within AfricaXchange to connect funders, practitioners, and policymakers and help shift development to locally led models.

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

urn:contentItem:6JGS-PP13-RRSD-W516-00000-00Quanta Services top gainer among Whitaker-Myers Wealth Managers holdings; jumps 35% in 31 days2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:00ZLexisNexisQuanta Services top gainer among Whitaker-Myers Wealth Managers holdings; jumps 35% in 31 days

NYSE STOCK REPORT

Quanta Services, Inc. (NYSE:PWR), is the top gainer by % price gain (out of its top 20 largest holdings by market cap), of Whitaker-Myers Wealth Managers, LTD. (CIK:0001973849) holdings (assuming there has been no significant change in the holdings since March 31, 2026).

PWR has soared $192.94 (or 35.1%) from $549.27 (as at March 31, 2026) to $742.21 as at today's close. Market Capitalization has surged by $28.7 billion from $81.9 billion to $110.6 billion in the past 31 days since March 31, 2026.

QUANTA SERVICES ACTIVITIES

Quanta Services, Inc., a leading provider of infrastructure solutions traded on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in engineering, procurement, construction, and maintenance services for the electric power, renewable energy, and pipeline industries. The company's core activities include designing, building, and maintaining critical infrastructure such as transmission lines, substations, and natural gas pipelines, serving utilities, telecommunications, and industrial clients across North America and select international markets. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects, particularly solar and wind farm installations, as well as energy storage and electric vehicle charging infrastructure, driven by global demand for sustainable energy and grid modernization. Quanta's diversified portfolio and emphasis on safety, innovation, and efficiency have solidified its position as a key partner in the evolving energy sector, supporting the transition to cleaner power sources. It is the NYSE's largest Construction company by market capitalisation.

25 Mar, 2026: Quanta Services quarterly dividends steady

Quanta Services today announced a quarterly dividend of 11.0c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 11.0c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, April 02, 2026 and the record date is Thursday, April 02, 2026 and it is payable on Friday, April 10. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended March 25 were 41.0c. This reflects actual dividend yield of 0.1% at the last price of $578.44.

09 Mar, 2026: Quanta Services to Host & Webcast its 2026 Investor Day

Quanta Services, Inc. (NYSE: PWR) announced that it will host and webcast its 2026 Investor Day from New York City on Tues., Mar. 31, 2026. Management intends to present certain information and engage in a question-and-answer discussion focused on the company's strategic initiatives and new long-term financial targets. The management presentation portion of the event is scheduled to begin at approximately 9:00 a.m. Eastern time and the entire event, including the question-and-answer portion, is expected to conclude by approximately 11:30 a.m. Eastern time.

Source: Company Website

20 Nov, 2025: Quanta Services quarterly dividends up 10% sequentially from previous quarter

Quanta Services today announced a quarterly dividend of 11.0c per share for Q4/2025; up 10% sequentially from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 10.0c. The ex-dividend date is Friday, January 02, 2026 and the record date is Friday, January 02, 2026 and it is payable on Monday, January 12, 2026. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended November 20 were 40.0c. This reflects actual dividend yield of 0.1% at the last price of $445.52.

urn:contentItem:6JHM-RY53-RV86-D54F-00000-00Infineon Technologies AG (OTC:IFNNY (ADR)) Earns Global Sustainability Recognition in Dow Jones Best-in-Class Indices Global and Europe2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisInfineon Technologies AG (OTC:IFNNY (ADR)) Earns Global Sustainability Recognition in Dow Jones Best-in-Class Indices Global and Europe

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 14:46 EDT

Infineon Technologies AG (OTC:IFNNY (ADR)) earns inclusion in the Dow Jones Best-in-Class Indices Global and Europe, as S&P Dow Jones Indices confirms Infineon’s placement on 6 May 2026 in New York for the 16th consecutive year. The recognition highlights Infineon’s ongoing ability to pair environmental performance with strong economic fundamentals.

Elke Reichart, Member of the Management Board and Chief Digital and Sustainability Officer, says, "We are very proud to be acknowledged as one of the world’s top sustainable companies. The listing for the 16th consecutive year demonstrates that we are consistently delivering on our ambition to be a role model in sustainability. At the same time, it serves as a strong encouragement for us to continue on this path. Our approach has always been clear: Environmental and economic sustainability must go hand in hand."

Infineon is advancing green and digital transformations across energy, safe mobility and energy efficient AI through its semiconductor solutions that serve as building blocks for the energy transition and climate friendly growth. In 2025, the company strengthened its sustainability program, with the Science Based Targets initiative recognizing its Scope 1 and 2 targets as aligned with the Paris Agreement 1.5°C goal and validating its Scope 3 target. Infineon also expanded collaboration with more than 100 suppliers through its Supplier Engagement Program and is implementing its Product Carbon Footprint initiative to increase transparency at the product level. The company remains committed to CO2 neutrality by 2030, a target set in 2020, and as of the end of fiscal year 2025 reduced emissions by 70 percent versus the 2019 baseline, with actual reductions exceeding 80 percent. Remaining emissions will be offset with high quality certificates.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 INFINEON TECHNOLOGIES ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Infineon Technologies Activities

Activities

Infineon Technologies AG, a leading German semiconductor company, specializes in the design, development, and manufacturing of microelectronics, including power semiconductors, sensors, and microcontrollers. Its core business activities focus on delivering innovative solutions for the automotive, industrial, and consumer electronics sectors, emphasizing energy efficiency, security, and connectivity. The company plays a pivotal role in enabling electrification and digital transformation across these markets. Among its fastest-growing segments are automotive applications, driven by the demand for electric vehicles and advanced driver-assistance systems; power management for renewable energy and industrial automation; and connectivity solutions for 5G and IoT devices. This strategic emphasis on high-growth areas positions Infineon as a key contributor to sustainable technologies, with a global presence supported by its extensive R&D and manufacturing network. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 3rd largest Semiconductors company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: Drew McKnight's Fortress Investment Group LLC : CPP Investments Announces Sale of European Non-Performing Loan Portfolio May 08, 2026

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ0-JKF3-RTJ0-H02F-00000-00Drew McKnight's Fortress Investment Group LLC: CPP Investments Announces Sale of European Non-Performing Loan Portfolio May 08, 20262026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisDrew McKnight's Fortress Investment Group LLC: CPP Investments Announces Sale of European Non-Performing Loan Portfolio May 08, 2026

TORONTO, Canada - May 07, 2026 - Canada Pension Plan Investment Board (CPP Investments) today announced that it has entered into an agreement to sell its remaining interests in its European non-performing loan ("NPL") portfolio to a newly formed joint venture vehicle established between funds managed by affiliates of Arrow Global Group ("Arrow") and funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group ("Fortress").

Source: Company Website

INDEX

SECTION 1 BIOGRAPHY: DREW MCKNIGHT Co-CEO & Managing Partner

SECTION 2 FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC PROFILE

SECTION 3 FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 BIOGRAPHY: DREW MCKNIGHT Co-CEO & Managing Partner

Mr. McKnight is Co-Chief Executive Officer and a Managing Partner based in Dallas. He serves on the Board of Directors, the Leadership Committee, and is a member of several investment committees.

Prior to joining Fortress in February 2005, Mr. McKnight worked at Fir Tree Partners where he was responsible for analyzing and trading high yield and convertible bonds, bank debt, derivatives and equities for the value-based hedge fund. Prior to Fir Tree, Mr. McKnight worked at Goldman, Sachs & Co. in Leveraged Finance and the Distressed Bank Debt trading group. Mr. McKnight serves on the Board of Trustees for the American Enterprise Institute and Board of the Overseers at the Hoover Institution. He is a member of the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas's Financial Sector Advisory Council; the Board of Advisors at UVA's Center for Politics and SMU's Cox School of Business Alternative Asset Management Center; and the UT Southwestern Medical Center President's Advisory Board. In addition, Mr. McKnight is a member of the Council on Foreign Relations.

Mr. McKnight received a B.A. in Economics from the University of Virginia.

SECTION 2 FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC PROFILE

2.1 ACTIVITIES

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

2.2 SUMMARY

value.Title: SolarEdge Technologies May Explore Opportunities Following Advanced Energy Industries New York False Claims Challenge *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-9093-SDNH-R2HR-00000-00SolarEdge Technologies May Explore Opportunities Following Advanced Energy Industries New York False Claims Challenge *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisSolarEdge Technologies May Explore Opportunities Following Advanced Energy Industries New York False Claims Challenge *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

What Stakeholders Need To Know:

This matter emphasizes the continuous review of product performance claims within publicly funded renewable energy initiatives. The litigation illustrates the potential liabilities that arise when equipment provided for state-supported installations does not meet required standards, leading to a more detailed evaluation of supply chain disclosures and warranty procedures throughout the solar equipment industry. The results may affect how manufacturers handle contractual duties associated with government funding, which could increase compliance expenses and modify procurement criteria for large-scale solar projects. Further industry impacts might involve an increased investor emphasis on quality assurance protocols and risk disclosures concerning emerging energy infrastructure. The situation demonstrates the intersection of state procurement regulations and private relator actions, which may promote more stringent internal testing and documentation procedures across the sector.

Background of the Case

The Otto Candies legal matter began in 2016 after the 2014 dissolution of Oceanografia S.A. de C.V., an oil-services contractor based in Mexico. Information indicates that Oceanografia submitted numerous falsified Pemex receivables to Grupo Financiero Banamex, a Citigroup subsidiary, which provided approximately $585 million against those invoices through a vendor-finance program. After Mexican authorities suspended Oceanografia in February 2014, Banamex recorded a $400 million loss. Institutional investors, such as hedge funds, insurance companies, and pension plans holding short-term certificates issued by Banamex, experienced principal losses of approximately $300 million. Plaintiffs allege that Citigroup Inc. and its New York-based senior executives knowingly participated in or recklessly ignored a multi-year operation, asserting violations of civil RICO statutes and the Securities Exchange Act. Although the case experienced multiple dismissals due to statute-of-limitations and extraterritoriality factors, the 11th Circuit overturned these decisions in September 2024, determining that sufficient domestic activities-including New York approvals, U.S. dollar wire transfers, and regulatory filings-took place to warrant domestic RICO application. Citigroup submitted a petition for review in November 2024, which is pending throughout 2025.

Competitive Impacts on SolarEdge Technologies

SolarEdge Technologies holds a steady market position in optimizer and inverter solutions for commercial and utility-scale solar projects. Public sentiment, as noted in industry reports, suggests that customers are placing greater emphasis on vendors with established long-term reliability records amid increased quality evaluations in government-funded projects. This situation may result in moderate adjustments to bid evaluations for municipal and educational solar programs, with projected revenue impacts staying below material levels.

Possible Opportunities for SolarEdge Technologies

Securing further participation in New York and Northeast municipal solar tenders where quality verification is assigned greater importance.

Broadening the cross-selling of monitoring platforms to clients requiring improved performance data transparency.

Developing partnerships with engineering, procurement, and construction organizations that prioritize public-sector compliance.

Advancing the integration of next-generation inverter technology presented as lower-risk options in state-funded procurement processes.

Why This Case Is Challenging for SolarEdge Technologies

Indirect effects from the litigation may increase sector-wide regulatory assessments of inverter performance claims in publicly funded initiatives. Added compliance documentation requirements could elevate administrative expenses for all market participants. The precedent set regarding false claims liability may lead to more conservative warranty terms across the industry, restricting flexibility during competitive bidding processes. Increased inquiries from investors and analysts concerning historical product quality could influence valuation multiples across the broader solar equipment sector.

Key Challenges for SolarEdge Technologies

Potential increases in third-party testing and certification costs for public procurement processes.

Increased focus on the disclosure of historical performance data during procurement evaluations.

Incremental adjustments to insurance premiums for product liability coverage.

Detailed observation of competitor litigation developments that could influence industry contracting standards.

Competitor's Strategic Responses

Implementation of standardized quality assurance reporting templates for all public-sector proposals.

Allocation of resources toward independent laboratory validation programs for primary product lines.

Modification of contractual representations to correspond with changing procurement requirements.

Improved training for sales personnel regarding regulatory compliance in government-funded initiatives.

Key Measures

SolarEdge Technologies is incorporating these measures through cross-functional working groups that align engineering, legal, and sales operations to maintain consistent application across its North American activities.

Possible Losses and Unexpected Advantages

Potential Advantages

Market share growth in quality-sensitive public projects, resulting in additional revenue projections of up to $40 million.

Enhanced brand perception as a dependable partner within regulated markets.

Expedited regulatory approval timelines for upcoming product iterations.

Broadened collaboration possibilities with public utilities requiring established vendors.

Potential Losses

Increased compliance and certification expenses projected at up to $15 million.

Operational delays in bid preparation resulting from supplementary documentation requirements.

Margin adjustments if clients require extended warranty coverage.

Additional legal review costs associated with contract language modifications.

Final Thoughts

SolarEdge Technologies encounters moderate indirect challenges from industry-wide quality evaluations but is positioned to gain from potential shifts toward vendors maintaining rigorous verification protocols. By continuing to allocate resources toward transparency and reliability, the organization can manage the developing procurement environment while utilizing opportunities in public-sector solar infrastructure projects.

Activities

Advanced Energy Industries Inc., a Nasdaq-listed company, specializes in designing, manufacturing, and supplying precision power conversion, measurement, and control solutions for high-tech industries. Its core business activities focus on enabling advanced manufacturing processes, including semiconductor fabrication, thin film deposition for solar panels and displays, and power management for data centers and industrial applications. The company leverages its expertise in power electronics to support energy-efficient technologies, serving sectors like telecommunications, life sciences, and automotive. Among its fastest-growing segments are data center infrastructure and renewable energy solutions, fueled by rising demand for AI-driven computing and sustainable power systems. This strategic emphasis on innovation and efficiency positions Advanced Energy as a key player in addressing global challenges in energy consumption and technological advancement. It is the NASDAQ's 3rd largest Semi equipment wafer company by market capitalisation.
