On the 1st of May, 2026, the Fund announced that applications are now open for the Milbank Fellows Program (MFP) and the Emerging Leaders Program (ELP), both designed to equip participants with vital knowledge and expertise.Set against the backdrop of evolving health policy challenges, the Milbank Memorial Fund is committed to fostering leadership that can effectively address pressing public health issues. The application period will remain open until June 19th, 2026, allowing ample time for qualified candidates to engage with these opportunities. The MFP targets senior leaders in state governments, while the ELP is geared toward rising leaders in state, large-county, and large-city governments.Participants in the Milbank programs will benefit from comprehensive coverage of all associated costs and will have the opportunity to collaborate with peers from various regions. The curriculum focuses on enhancing leadership capabilities, understanding complex health policy topics, and improving participants' ability to implement impactful health policies in their communities. Milbank aims to create diverse cohorts that reflect a broad spectrum of political and geographic backgrounds.The selection criteria for the Milbank programs emphasize a commitment to health improvement and a passion for addressing health disparities. Candidates should possess roles of influence within legislative or executive branches of government and demonstrate a willingness to learn and share expertise on population health policy. The Milbank Memorial Fund invites government officials to apply or nominate exceptional individuals who meet these qualifications.For more details about the Milbank Fellows Program and the Emerging Leaders Program, as well as application guidelines, interested parties are encouraged to visit the respective program pages on the Milbank website. This initiative underscores the Fund's dedication to advancing public health leadership at a time when effective policies are more crucial than ever.

+ MILBANK TRAINEE SETS NEW WORLD RECORD IN MARATHON DRESSED AS CAPTAIN UNDERPANTS

A trainee solicitor at US law firm Milbank has successfully broken a Guinness World Record at the London Marathon, completing the race dressed as Captain Underpants in just 2 hours and 27 minutes. The event took place on 28th April 2026, and Hunt's impressive time not only secured him the record but also surpassed the previous benchmark of 2 hours and 42 minutes for the fastest marathon dressed as a book character.Archie Hunt, who trained rigorously for months while balancing a demanding role at Milbank, achieved this remarkable feat while wearing a full Captain Underpants costume. His preparation included early morning runs of over 20 kilometers, showcasing his dedication to both his legal career and his athletic pursuits. The world record attempt also adhered to strict guidelines set by Guinness World Records, ensuring that official timing data, witness statements, and photographic evidence were in place to validate the achievement.The marathon held personal significance for Hunt, as he was fundraising for Evelina London Children’s Charity, which is affiliated with the hospital where he receives treatment for dilated cardiomyopathy. Despite this condition potentially adding 10 to 15 minutes to his marathon times, Hunt's determination propelled him through the race, allowing him to finish in the top 0.1% of participants at a World Major marathon.In an inspiring statement, Hunt expressed, “It feels incredible to have broken the costume record and to now be the fastest recorded marathoner diagnosed with dilated cardiomyopathy.” He emphasized the importance of raising awareness for DCM, hoping that his accomplishment would inspire others facing similar health challenges. Hunt's achievement has since garnered attention from brands offering sponsorships and even podcast invitations, as he looks forward to future races, including a 110km ultra in Finland scheduled for June.Hunt’s remarkable journey serves as a testament to the idea that one can pursue excellence in both professional and athletic endeavors. His combination of humor, grit, and determination has not only broken records but also highlighted the strength of the human spirit, even amid personal challenges. With a goal of achieving a sub-2:20 marathon time in the future, Hunt continues to inspire both his colleagues at Milbank and the wider community.

+ MILBANK'S STRATEGIC PLAN TO COMBAT INVASIVE BEETLE THREATENS ASH TREES

MILBANK — In a proactive response to an invasive species threat, city leaders in Milbank are implementing a comprehensive strategy to protect their ash trees from the emerald ash borer. The infestation, first detected last summer, poses a significant risk to the northeastern South Dakota city’s tree canopy, which is composed of over 50% ash trees, including those found in local parks and the cemetery. On 21st April 2026, city administrator Steve Pendergrass outlined the financial implications of the crisis, indicating that the cost of removing the trees could reach up to $4 million.Conversely, Milbank's strategy to develop what it describes as 'herd immunity' against the beetle will require an estimated $2.4 million over the next 12 years. This approach aims to enhance the resilience of the ash trees through targeted treatment and monitoring, allowing the city to preserve its green spaces while combating the invasive pest. The strategy is seen as a viable model that could be adapted by other cities in South Dakota facing similar threats to their tree populations.The emerald ash borer, a woodboring beetle, has wreaked havoc across various regions of the United States, leading to widespread tree loss. Milbank's initiative to promote herd immunity reflects an understanding of the ecological importance of maintaining a robust tree canopy, which provides numerous environmental benefits including improved air quality, water retention, and habitat for wildlife. By investing in the health of these trees, Milbank hopes to create a sustainable urban forest that can withstand the challenges posed by invasive species.As Milbank embarks on this ambitious project, the collaboration between local officials, environmental experts, and community members will be crucial. The city plans to implement a series of treatments on a phased basis, focusing on the most vulnerable areas first. This careful approach not only aims to save the existing trees but also hopes to foster a greater awareness of the importance of tree health and biodiversity in urban environments.The successful execution of Milbank's strategy could serve as a template for other communities grappling with similar challenges. As the city takes steps to protect its ash trees, it stands as a testament to the importance of proactive environmental stewardship, offering a beacon of hope for cities that wish to maintain their natural heritage in the face of invasive threats.

DASHBOARD: DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP

PermID4296380340Websitehttp://www.davispolk.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress450 Lexington Avenue New York, NY 10017 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Davis Polk & Wardwell LLP, founded in 1849, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 1,000 lawyers across 10 offices, it serves clients in finance, technology, and healthcare, specializing in M&A, capital markets, and regulatory matters. In 2020, it advised on Morgan Stanleys $13 billion E*Trade acquisition and Gilead Sciences $21 billion Immunomedics deal. In 2024, it expanded its Menlo Park office with key hires like Paul Scrivano from Ropes & Gray. Generating over $1.5 billion annually, its culture emphasizes collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work. Competing with Cravath, Davis Polk stands out for its dealmaking prowess, ranking second in 2020 M&A league tables. Recent initiatives include AI-driven legal tech and ESG advisory services.

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIGENERGY'S HK IPO GAINS MOMENTUM WITH DAVIS POLK'S LEGAL EXPERTISE

Sigenergy Technology, a Chinese energy storage systems maker, has successfully navigated its initial public offering (IPO) in Hong Kong, raising HK$4.4 billion, equivalent to approximately $562 million. The transaction was facilitated by prominent law firms Davis Polk & Wardwell and Jia Yuan Law Offices, who provided critical legal advice throughout the process. This significant event was recorded on the 17th of April 2026.Founded in May 2022, Sigenergy specializes in distributed energy storage systems (DESS) and is headquartered in Shanghai. The company focuses on delivering innovative stackable, distributed solar-plus-storage all-in-one solutions, positioning itself strategically within the rapidly growing energy sector. Sigenergy has emerged as a leader in the market, commanding a 28.6 percent share in the global segment of stackable all-in-one distributed energy storage solutions, according to market data.The IPO was underwritten by a consortium of financial institutions, including CITIC Securities and BNP Paribas, with China International Capital Corporation (CICC) also playing a role as a joint sponsor. Legal support for the underwriters was provided by Linklaters and Jingtian & Gongcheng, with Linklaters’ team led by partner Christine Xu, who oversees Greater China ECM, and capital markets partner Lipton Li, alongside counsel Queenie Tong.In a related development, Mumbai-based Mindspace Business Parks REIT has made headlines with its acquisition of the International Tech Park in Chennai, valued at 30 billion rupees, or $321 million. This transaction was advised by Khaitan & Co, marking a significant investment in the Indian real estate sector. Furthermore, Indian asset manager SBI Funds Management is preparing for a substantial 130-billion-rupee IPO, with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas and Linklaters advising on the endeavor.The successful IPO of Sigenergy Technology not only highlights the company’s robust market position but also reflects the growing investor interest in renewable energy solutions. As the demand for energy storage systems continues to rise, Sigenergy is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the sector.

+ DAVIS POLK'S LIGHTYEAR CAPITAL SECURES $2.5B FOR SIXTH FUND

Lightyear Capital, a private equity firm co-founded by Davis Polk & Wardwell, has successfully raised $2.5 billion for its sixth fund. This significant capital infusion highlights the firm's robust position in the asset management space and reflects investor confidence in its investment strategy and market outlook. The announcement was made public on April 14, 2026.The latest fund is poised to target investments in the financial services and technology sectors, which have shown resilience and growth potential in recent years. With this capital, Lightyear Capital aims to capitalize on emerging opportunities within these industries, leveraging its extensive network and expertise to identify lucrative investment avenues. Davis Polk's longstanding experience in providing legal services to financial institutions further strengthens Lightyear's operational capabilities.The fundraising comes amid a competitive landscape for private equity firms, where securing investor commitments has become increasingly challenging. Lightyear Capital's ability to attract significant backing can be attributed to its track record of strong returns and strategic investment decisions. The firm plans to focus on both established companies and innovative startups, providing them with the necessary support to scale their operations effectively.Davis Polk & Wardwell's involvement in Lightyear Capital is not merely advisory; the firm's attorneys have been integral in structuring the fund and navigating the regulatory landscape. Their expertise ensures that Lightyear operates within legal parameters while maximizing investor returns, a critical factor for stakeholders in today’s complex financial environment. The collaboration between the two entities underscores the synergy between legal prowess and investment acumen.Looking ahead, investors will be keen to see how Lightyear Capital deploys its newly raised capital and whether it can maintain its momentum in generating returns. As the market evolves, the firm’s strategies will be closely scrutinized, particularly in the context of economic fluctuations and sector-specific challenges. With Davis Polk's backing, Lightyear Capital is well-positioned to make a significant impact in the private equity arena.

+ DAVIS POLK STRENGTHENS WEST COAST PRESENCE WITH LA OFFICE LAUNCH

Davis Polk & Wardwell is set to establish a new office in Los Angeles, tapping into the expertise of Jason Russell, the head of Skadden Arps Slate Meagher & Flom's LA office, to lead this expansion. The firm announced this strategic move on 14th April 2026, reinforcing its commitment to grow its influence in key markets.This expansion represents a significant step for Davis Polk, a firm renowned for its legal excellence. By bringing onboard Russell, a seasoned litigator with a robust portfolio of high-profile clients including the NBA, Christie's, and MGA Entertainment, the firm aims to solidify its footing in the lucrative Los Angeles legal landscape.Jason Russell, who has also served on Skadden's policy committee, is expected to play a pivotal role in establishing Davis Polk's presence in LA. His leadership is anticipated to attract additional talent as the firm plans to make further hires for the new office, enhancing its capabilities in the region.Davis Polk's move into Los Angeles not only showcases its ambition to broaden its geographic footprint but also highlights its strategy to compete with other major law firms in California. The firm's focus on high-profile cases and clients could translate into significant opportunities as it seeks to capitalize on the vibrant legal market.As Davis Polk prepares for its Los Angeles office's opening, the legal community will be watching closely. The firm's ability to leverage Russell's extensive experience and connections may position it favorably among clients seeking top-tier legal representation in one of the nation’s most competitive markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES UNPRECEDENTED REVENUE GROWTH IN LONDON

Debevoise & Plimpton has reported a remarkable revenue surge of 40% in its London office, reaching a record $268 million. This significant increase has drawn attention in the legal industry and among investors seeking insights into the firm's growth trajectory. The results, announced on 7th April 2026, highlight the firm's robust performance in a competitive market.The leap in revenue is attributed to an uptick in demand for legal services across various sectors, including corporate law and dispute resolution. Debevoise & Plimpton's strategic focus on high-value client engagements and its ability to adapt to changing market conditions have played a key role in this growth. The firm has consistently positioned itself as a leader in providing comprehensive legal solutions to its clientele.In light of this impressive performance, analysts are keenly observing how Debevoise & Plimpton plans to sustain this momentum moving forward. The firm has expressed its commitment to investing in talent and expanding its service offerings, which may further bolster its market position. This proactive approach could potentially attract more clients and drive additional revenue growth in the coming years.The legal sector has witnessed a notable transformation in recent years, and firms like Debevoise & Plimpton that embrace innovation and adapt to new challenges may be well-positioned for continued success. As competition intensifies, maintaining a strong financial performance will be crucial for the firm to retain its competitive edge.As Debevoise & Plimpton celebrates this milestone, the legal community is left wondering whether this growth trend will continue. Investors and clients alike will be looking for signs of sustained performance, as the firm navigates the complexities of the ever-evolving legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES LANDMARK VICTORY FOR YPF S.A. IN $16 BILLION CASE

In a significant legal victory, Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has successfully secured an affirmance from the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit regarding its summary judgment win on behalf of YPF S.A., the largest energy producer in Argentina. This decision, made on 30th March 2026, effectively dismisses claims for over $16 billion in damages and interest, marking a pivotal moment in a case that has lingered in the Southern District ofNew York since 2015.The Second Circuit upheld the District Court’s finding that YPF did not have any contractual obligations to Petersen Energ a Inversora, S.A.U., Petersen Energ a, S.A.U., or Eton Park Capital Management, L.P., along with its associated funds. This ruling is seen as a major victory for YPF, affirming its non-liability for the claims advanced by the plaintiffs, who also included litigation funder Burford Capital.The case traces its roots back to Argentina's 2012 intervention and subsequent expropriation of 51% of YPF's capital stock. The litigation commenced in 2015, and Debevoise took over YPF's defense in December 2020 after earlier motions to dismiss were denied. Following two years of extensive discovery involving numerous expert testimonies, both the plaintiffs and YPF, alongside the Republic of Argentina, sought summary judgment in 2022.In 2023, the District Court granted YPF’s motion for summary judgment in its entirety, adopting Debevoise's arguments that YPF was not obliged under its bylaws to compel the Republic to conduct a tender offer or restrict the Republic's corporate governance rights over the expropriated shares. Conversely, the District Court largely granted the plaintiffs’ motion for summary judgment against the Republic, resulting in a judgment exceeding $16 billion against it in September 2023.Following appeals from all parties involved, the case was fully briefed and argued before the Second Circuit in October 2025. The affirmation of YPF's victory not only reinforces the firm's position but also highlights the complexities involved in international energy disputes and expropriation cases, setting a notable precedent in the legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE ELEVATES JEFFREY SULLIVAN KC AND INA POPOVA TO EUROPEAN DISPUTES LEADERSHIP

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has announced the appointment of partners Jeffrey Sullivan KC and Ina Popova as Co-Chairs of European Disputes, effective immediately. This strategic move enhances the firm's presence in key markets, particularly in Paris, Frankfurt, and Luxembourg. The announcement comes on the 17th of March, 2026, highlighting the firm's commitment to strengthening its capabilities in international dispute resolution across mainland Europe.Catherine Amirfar, who serves as Co-Chair of Debevoise's global International Dispute Resolution Group, expressed confidence in the leadership of Sullivan KC and Popova. She stated, 'Ina and Jeff’s leadership will build on Debevoise’s long-standing strength in major disputes across mainland Europe, ensuring we continue to deliver the highest level of advocacy and strategic counsel to our clients.' Their appointments align with the firm's strategy of appointing regional leaders to drive its dispute resolution practice worldwide, joining other prominent leaders in Latin America and Asia.Jeffrey Sullivan KC is already recognized as a leading figure in international arbitration and public international law. In his new role, he will continue to focus on high-stakes commercial and investor-state arbitrations, representing a diverse clientele that includes companies and private equity sponsors. His established reputation in the market is complemented by his experience as an arbitrator in significant cases.Ina Popova brings a wealth of experience in international arbitration and public international law, focusing on managing and litigating complex disputes. Her dual qualification in civil and common law, along with her fluency in multiple languages, positions her uniquely to navigate the intricacies of cross-border disputes. Popova's previous role as a member of the ICC International Court of Arbitration further underscores her expertise and credibility in the field.With over four decades of experience, Debevoise’s International Dispute Resolution Group has consistently delivered exceptional outcomes in international arbitration and public international law. The team's extensive expertise spans multiple jurisdictions and languages, making it a formidable player in the global disputes arena. Recent engagements have involved over 125 countries and stakes exceeding $110 billion, showcasing Debevoise's commitment to achieving significant results for its clients.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [2 News That Matters]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL'S RODGE COHEN REFLECTS ON FIVE DECADES IN BANKING

H. Rodgin “Rodge” Cohen, senior chair at Sullivan & Cromwell, has been a transformative force in the banking industry for over five decades. On 19th February 2026, during an event hosted by NYU Law's Institute for Corporate Governance & Finance (ICGF), Cohen shared his experiences and insights on the evolution of the modern financial system. The conversation was enriched by contributions from Jay Clayton, former chairman of the US Securities and Exchange Commission, and Edward Rock, co-director of ICGF.Cohen's legal career has coincided with numerous landmark events that have shaped the financial landscape. Clayton, who has previously worked alongside Cohen at Sullivan & Cromwell, highlighted the profound changes in banking regulation since the 1970s. Cohen emphasized the significance of the Bank Secrecy Act and the Community Reinvestment Act, noting that these measures have drastically changed access to banking services, reducing the percentage of unbanked Americans from one-third to just five percent.A defining moment in Cohen's career came during the 2008 global financial crisis, where he represented several major banks in efforts to stabilize the financial system. Reflecting on the crisis, Cohen recalled the critical hours leading up to the government’s decision to intervene after the collapse of Lehman Brothers. He stated, 'We were on the edge of a cliff,' emphasizing the urgency and gravity of the situation. His efforts during this tumultuous time solidified his reputation as a leading financial services lawyer.Cohen's influence extends beyond crisis management; he has also played a crucial role in regulatory changes that have transformed the banking industry. For instance, his advocacy for interstate banking in the 1980s allowed banks to expand their operations across state lines, significantly reshaping the banking landscape. Clayton praised Cohen’s ability to interpret statutes creatively, which has been vital for navigating the complexities of financial regulation.As the conversation turned to future challenges, Cohen highlighted the importance of effective cryptocurrency regulation. He warned of potential pitfalls if regulations do not keep pace with the evolving market, likening the situation to Gresham’s Law. 'Bad regulation drives out good regulation,' he cautioned, stressing the need for robust frameworks to manage the risks associated with cryptocurrency. Cohen's insights serve as a reminder of the ongoing evolution of the financial sector and the critical role of firms like Sullivan & Cromwell in shaping its future.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL EXPANDS LONDON FINANCE TEAM WITH NEW PARTNERS

Sullivan & Cromwell LLP has announced the addition of Christopher McLaughlin and Alastair McVeigh as partners in its London office. This strategic move underscores the firm’s commitment to bolstering its private capital capabilities in the UK and European markets. The announcement follows the date of 21st January 2026, marking a significant step in the firm’s expansion plans.Christopher McLaughlin will spearhead the firm's private equity sponsor and borrower acquisition, as well as leveraged finance practices in London. Meanwhile, Alastair McVeigh will lead the European private credit offering, focusing on direct and specialty lending. Robert Giuffra and Scott Miller, Co-Chairs of Sullivan & Cromwell, expressed enthusiasm for the new hires, noting that their expertise will enhance the firm's ability to serve private equity sponsors, lenders, and private credit providers across the region.McLaughlin is recognized as a leading figure in acquisition and leveraged finance law within Europe, boasting extensive experience in advising private equity sponsors and private credit providers. His accolades include being ranked as a Leading Individual for Leveraged Finance by Chambers UK and a Leading Partner for Acquisition Finance by Legal 500. Similarly, McVeigh is well-respected for his work in leveraged finance, particularly in private credit and specialty lending, positioning him as a valuable asset to Sullivan & Cromwell’s growing practice.The co-managing partners of Sullivan & Cromwell's London office, Kon Asimacopoulos and John Horsfield-Bradbury, highlighted that the arrivals of McLaughlin and McVeigh are integral to the execution of the firm's private capital strategy. They emphasized that the new partners’ top-tier expertise aligns seamlessly with the firm’s global platform and its established finance practice in the U.S.Sullivan & Cromwell's Private Equity Practice is known for representing top financial sponsors and private capital investors in complex transactions globally. With an average deal size of $2.3 billion for completed European private equity transactions in 2024, according to Mergermarket, the firm’s reputation for excellence continues to grow. The addition of McLaughlin and McVeigh is expected to further enhance the firm’s capabilities across the spectrum of private credit and leveraged finance, solidifying its position as a leader in these sectors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB [3 News That Matters]

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB MAINTAINS DOMINANCE AMONG LATIN AMERICA'S TOP BANKING ADVISERS

Cleary Gottlieb has once again been named the most popular international firm advising Latin America’s 100 largest banks, as reported by the Latin American Corporate Counsel Association (LACCA). The latest research into Who Represents Latin America’s Biggest Banks? indicates that as of 6th May 2026, Cleary Gottlieb has the highest number of clients, with 37 in its portfolio, underscoring its unmatched standing in this competitive landscape.In addition to its strong showing in the banking sector, Cleary Gottlieb was also recognized as a leading international firm in LACCA’s study of the most favored law firms for Latin America’s Top 100 companies by revenue. The firm represents 51 of the largest corporates operating in the region, highlighting the extensive reach and expertise of Cleary Gottlieb across various industries and geographical borders.The consistent recognition of Cleary Gottlieb by LACCA across both banking and corporate categories reflects a long-standing track record of excellence that has characterized its presence in the Latin American market for years. This enduring reputation serves as a testament to the firm's dedication to providing top-tier legal services tailored to the needs of its clients in the region.As Cleary Gottlieb continues to navigate the complexities of the Latin American legal landscape, its ability to maintain such a strong client base is indicative of its strategic insight and deep understanding of local and international market dynamics. The firm’s commitment to fostering relationships with key financial institutions and corporations has undoubtedly positioned it as a leader in the sector.For further details on LACCA’s research and insights regarding the legal landscape in Latin America, interested parties are encouraged to review the findings, which are available upon subscription.

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB WELCOMES JENNIFER MORTON TO FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS GROUP

Cleary Gottlieb has announced the addition of Jennifer Morton as a partner in its New York office, enhancing its award-winning financial institutions practice. This development was confirmed on the 7th of May 2026, marking a significant step in the firm's ongoing efforts to bolster its capabilities in financial services regulation.Managing Partner Jeff Karpf expressed enthusiasm about Morton’s arrival, stating, "Jennifer is a great addition to the Cleary team. As a widely recognized leader in broker-dealer regulation, Jennifer’s arrival significantly deepens our ability to deliver comprehensive financial services regulatory advice to clients around the world." This move underscores Cleary Gottlieb’s commitment to investing in key areas that align with client needs.With over 20 years of experience, Morton brings extensive expertise in broker-dealer regulation and transactions. Her advisory practice encompasses a variety of complex regulatory and transactional matters, including public and private mergers and acquisitions, SEC-registered offerings, and the evolving landscape of digital assets and blockchain technology. Hugh Conroy, Jr., who leads the financial institutions practice at Cleary Gottlieb, noted that Morton’s insights will be invaluable in addressing issues related to U.S. market entry and regulatory compliance.Morton's recognition as one of the nation’s leading financial services lawyers by Chambers USA highlights her deep understanding of complex market regulations. In 2024, she was honored with the Women in Business Law Financial Regulation Lawyer of the Year Award from the International Financial Law Review, a testament to her contributions to the field. "I am thrilled to join Cleary Gottlieb," Morton stated, emphasizing the firm’s strong platform for addressing clients’ most intricate legal challenges in a dynamic regulatory landscape.Morton is the second financial institutions partner to join Cleary Gottlieb in recent weeks. On April 20, Igor Kleyman was welcomed to the practice, focusing on regulatory and transactional matters pertaining to digital assets and fintech. These strategic additions come on the heels of two senior capital markets partners joining the firm earlier this year, reinforcing Cleary Gottlieb’s position as a leader in the financial services sector.

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB STRENGTHENS LONDON OFFICE WITH NEW PARTNER HIRE

Cleary Gottlieb announced that David Christmas will join the firm’s London office as a partner in its top-tier Global Funds practice starting May 5th, 2026. This move marks the firm’s seventh partner hire this year, underscoring its commitment to expanding its capabilities and presence in the competitive legal market.David Christmas brings a wealth of experience in fund formation across various alternative asset classes and has a notable background in GP-led secondary transactions. Tihir Sarkar, a partner in London and member of the Executive Committee, emphasized that the continued expansion of Cleary Gottlieb’s global funds and private capital capabilities remains a principal strategic priority. The firm’s London office has seen significant growth, with its partner headcount doubling since 2021, and the momentum is expected to continue.Cleary Gottlieb's Global Funds team is recognized as Band 1 for Fund Formation in the U.S. by Chambers, having advised on funds with commitments exceeding $500 billion worldwide over its 30-year history. Managing Partner Jeff Karpf highlighted the complexity and sophistication of the transactions the team handles, reinforcing the firm’s strong market position. The addition of Christmas is seen as a valuable enhancement to the funds platform, which integrates transatlantic, Asian, and bicoastal U.S. coverage.David Christmas expressed enthusiasm about joining Cleary Gottlieb, noting the firm’s collaborative platform and its extensive expertise across key jurisdictions and specialties. His recognition in Law.com International’s 2022 list of Private Equity Rising Stars and as a Leading Partner in Investment Funds: Private Funds by the Legal 500 further solidifies his credentials. The London office's growth trajectory follows a record-breaking year in 2025, which saw the promotion and hiring of a combined 41 partners globally across various practice areas, including corporate, funds, and litigation.As Cleary Gottlieb continues to attract top talent and expand its offerings, the firm remains well-positioned to meet the evolving needs of its asset management clients who are diversifying their strategies across private equity, credit, real assets, and secondaries. With David Christmas onboard, the firm's Global Funds practice is set to strengthen its market leadership further.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ LATHAM & WATKINS FACILITATES PAREX RESOURCES' $500 MILLION NOTE PLACEMENT

Latham &Watkins has announced its role in advising on the financing arrangement involving Calgary-based Parex Resources Inc. The law firm represented the financing sources in the private placement of $500 million in 8.50% five-year notes, with the transaction officially recorded on 11th May 2026.The proceeds from these notes will be allocated towards acquiring the exploration and production assets held by Frontera Energy Corporation in Colombia. This acquisition marks a significant step for Parex, one of the largest independent oil and gas companies in the country, as it aims to enhance its sustainable, conventional production capabilities.Frontera Energy Corporation, a Canadian public entity, specializes in the exploration, development, production, transportation, storage, and sale of oil and natural gas across South America. The acquisition encompasses Frontera’s entire Colombian upstream business, which includes key assets such as its oil and gas exploration and production facilities, a reverse osmosis water treatment facility, and a palm oil plantation.Latham & Watkins played a crucial role in this transaction, advising the initial purchasers on the senior notes offering and representing the financing parties in their commitments to support the acquisition. The corporate deal team, led by partners David Miller, Roderick Branch, and Sam Rettew, along with counsel Manasi Bhattacharyya, was instrumental in structuring the financing.In addition to corporate advisory, Latham & Watkins provided expertise on tax matters through partner Bryant Lee and associate Lucas Migliano, as well as environmental considerations led by partner Joshua Marnitz and associate Nolan Fargo. This multifaceted legal support underscores Latham’s comprehensive approach to facilitating complex financial transactions in the energy sector.

+ LUMEN TECHNOLOGIES PURSUES GROWTH WITH ALKIRA ACQUISITION

Lumen Technologies, Inc. (NYSE: LUMN) has entered into an agreement to acquire Alkira, a cloud-native, carrier-agnostic networking platform, in a transaction valued at US$475 million. This all-cash acquisition aims to enhance Lumen's capabilities in providing connectivity and network services across hybrid and multi-cloud environments. The deal is anticipated to close in the third quarter of 2026, subject to customary regulatory approvals and closing conditions.Latham & Watkins LLP is advising Lumen on this transaction, with a corporate deal team led by partners Bradley Faris, based in Chicago and New York. The firm has assembled a comprehensive team to address various aspects of the acquisition, including compensation and benefits, labor and employment, intellectual property, data privacy, antitrust, tax, and environmental matters, showcasing its extensive expertise in complex corporate transactions.Alkira's platform is designed to facilitate enterprises in designing, deploying, and managing their connectivity across varied cloud environments. By acquiring Alkira, Lumen aims to strengthen its position in the cloud networking space, enabling more efficient and flexible network solutions for its customers. This strategic move aligns with Lumen's ongoing efforts to adapt to the rapidly evolving technology landscape and meet the demands of enterprise clients.The acquisition not only signifies Lumen's commitment to expanding its service offerings but also reflects the growing trend of consolidation within the technology sector, particularly in cloud services. As businesses increasingly migrate to cloud environments, the need for robust networking solutions becomes paramount. Lumen's investment in Alkira underscores its vision of providing comprehensive solutions that cater to modern enterprise needs.As the transaction moves towards closure, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the regulatory process and the integration of Alkira's technologies into Lumen's existing infrastructure. With Latham & Watkins at the helm of legal advisory, Lumen appears well-positioned to navigate the complexities of this acquisition and capitalize on the opportunities that lie ahead in the cloud networking market.

+ LATHAM & WATKINS AND LOEB & LOEB LEAD AI BATTERY FIRM IN $250M SPAC DEAL

Latham & Watkins and Loeb & Loeb have taken leading roles in the $250 million merger of AI Battery Company with a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC). This transaction is poised to bolster the AI Battery Company's financial resources and accelerate its growth trajectory in the burgeoning market for artificial intelligence-driven battery technologies. The merger is expected to close shortly, following regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions.The deal, announced on 21st April 2026, marks a significant milestone for AI Battery Company, which is focused on developing advanced battery solutions that leverage artificial intelligence for improved efficiency and sustainability. The merger with the SPAC aims to provide the necessary capital infusion to enhance research and development efforts, as well as to expand the company's manufacturing capabilities.Latham & Watkins has been instrumental in navigating the complex legal landscape associated with SPAC transactions, drawing on its extensive experience in capital markets and mergers and acquisitions. The firm’s involvement underscores its commitment to supporting innovative companies in the technology sector as they seek to access public markets. Loeb & Loeb's expertise complements Latham’s efforts, particularly in regulatory compliance and corporate governance matters.Investors are keenly observing the progress of AI Battery Company's merger, as the demand for AI-integrated solutions continues to rise. The company's approach to battery technology positions it at the forefront of a rapidly evolving industry, where efficiency and sustainability are critical. The SPAC merger not only provides a pathway to public listing but also enhances the company’s visibility within the investment community.As the transaction nears completion, stakeholders are optimistic about the future of AI Battery Company in the context of an increasingly competitive market. The legal guidance from Latham & Watkins and Loeb & Loeb is expected to play a crucial role in ensuring a smooth transition into public ownership, setting the stage for further growth and innovation in the AI battery space.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P. [3 News That Matters]

+ SKADDEN STRENGTHENS M&A PRACTICE WITH DREW BARBER'S RETURN

Skadden has announced the return of Drew Barber as a partner in its M&A practice at the Boston office. Most recently, Mr. Barber served as senior vice president and general counsel of a multinational aerospace and defense company, where he oversaw strategic acquisitions and managed a team of approximately 1,000 professionals. This significant appointment was made public on 6th May 2026.Mr. Barber's extensive experience encompasses advising clients on a wide range of corporate matters, including mergers, acquisitions, divestitures, corporate governance, and securities law. Over the years, he has handled complex public and private transactions across various industries such as aerospace and defense, manufacturing, technology, financial services, and real estate. His background positions him well to support Skadden's diverse clientele.Allison Schneirov, global head of Skadden’s transactions practices, expressed enthusiasm about Mr. Barber’s return, stating, "He brings a powerful combination of strategic M&A experience, trusted counsel to executive leadership, and a genuine understanding of what matters most to in-house legal teams." This sentiment was echoed by Moshe Spinowitz, head of Skadden’s Boston office, who highlighted the value of Mr. Barber's industry knowledge and in-house perspective.In his own words, Mr. Barber articulated his excitement about rejoining Skadden, emphasizing the firm's global platform and talent depth. He noted, "After years of advising on high-stakes business and legal matters as a general counsel, I am excited to bring that firsthand perspective to our clients as they navigate increasingly complex transactions and regulatory challenges."Mr. Barber previously served as an associate at Skadden, where he advised clients on M&A, capital markets, and corporate governance matters. He earned his J.D. from the University of Connecticut School of Law and holds an undergraduate degree from Trinity College. Skadden’s commitment to delivering innovative legal solutions continues to strengthen with the addition of talent like Mr. Barber.

+ SKADDEN ELEVATES NINE PARTNERS IN LONDON AMID NEW PROMOTION MODEL

Skadden has promoted nine partners in London as part of a 55-strong global partner round for the US firm. This year’s round is understood to be the firm’s first since it rolled out a non-equity partner tier.The New York heavyweight recently introduced a non-equity tier, joining a growing number of US elite firms updating their compensation structures to retain talent and control the size of their equity ranks. This year’s global cohort is more than double last year’s 22, with the London intake up significantly from the firm’s typically small City rounds.The nine new London partners are Jonathan Benson in White Collar Defense and Investigations, Zoe Q. Cooper Sutton in Finance, Benjamin T. Davies in Corporate, Kara L. Heggs in Tax, Jason Hewitt and Nicola Kerr-Shaw in National Security, Feargal Ryan in Financial Institutions, Kenneth J. Ryan in Capital Markets, and Cristina Tomassini in Corporate.Skadden has historically taken a conservative approach to promotions, particularly in London. Over the past four years, the firm promoted just two partners in the City annually, with three in 2021 and only one in 2020. The expanded round comes as top US firms increasingly adopt non-equity tiers—a model designed to improve retention and create more flexibility in partnership structures.Skadden’s London office has also traditionally been selective on lateral hiring, although it made a notable move earlier this year with the hire of disputes partners Sophie Lamb KC and Samuel Papef from Latham. Skadden was approached for comment but had not responded at the time of publication.

+ SKADDEN GUIDES OTSUKA IN USD1.2 BILLION BIOTECH ACQUISITION

+ ELON MUSK'S X CORP ENDS $90M LEGAL BATTLE WITH WACHTELL LIPTON

The firm emphasized its 'complete success' after months of litigation, which ultimately secured billions for shareholders. A spokesperson for Wachtell Lipton expressed satisfaction with the outcome, stating, 'We are pleased that X has dismissed its meritless lawsuit with prejudice. There was no settlement.' This marks a significant turn in the ongoing legal saga surrounding the Twitter acquisition.This legal retreat comes on the heels of another reconciliation for X Corp, which recently acknowledged a $128 million executive payout suit related to the tumultuous period during the Twitter acquisition. + MILBANK TRAINEE SETS NEW WORLD RECORD IN MARATHON DRESSED AS CAPTAIN UNDERPANTS

A trainee solicitor at US law firm Milbank has successfully broken a Guinness World Record at the London Marathon, completing the race dressed as Captain Underpants in just 2 hours and 27 minutes. The event took place on 28th April 2026, and Hunt's impressive time not only secured him the record but also surpassed the previous benchmark of 2 hours and 42 minutes for the fastest marathon dressed as a book character.Archie Hunt, who trained rigorously for months while balancing a demanding role at Milbank, achieved this remarkable feat while wearing a full Captain Underpants costume. His preparation included early morning runs of over 20 kilometers, showcasing his dedication to both his legal career and his athletic pursuits. The world record attempt also adhered to strict guidelines set by Guinness World Records, ensuring that official timing data, witness statements, and photographic evidence were in place to validate the achievement.The marathon held personal significance for Hunt, as he was fundraising for Evelina London Children’s Charity, which is affiliated with the hospital where he receives treatment for dilated cardiomyopathy. Despite this condition potentially adding 10 to 15 minutes to his marathon times, Hunt's determination propelled him through the race, allowing him to finish in the top 0.1% of participants at a World Major marathon.In an inspiring statement, Hunt expressed, “It feels incredible to have broken the costume record and to now be the fastest recorded marathoner diagnosed with dilated cardiomyopathy.” He emphasized the importance of raising awareness for DCM, hoping that his accomplishment would inspire others facing similar health challenges. Hunt's achievement has since garnered attention from brands offering sponsorships and even podcast invitations, as he looks forward to future races, including a 110km ultra in Finland scheduled for June.Hunt’s remarkable journey serves as a testament to the idea that one can pursue excellence in both professional and athletic endeavors. With a goal of achieving a sub-2:20 marathon time in the future, Hunt continues to inspire both his colleagues at Milbank and the wider community.

+ MILBANK'S STRATEGIC PLAN TO COMBAT INVASIVE BEETLE THREATENS ASH TREES

MILBANK — In a proactive response to an invasive species threat, city leaders in Milbank are implementing a comprehensive strategy to protect their ash trees from the emerald ash borer. The infestation, first detected last summer, poses a significant risk to the northeastern South Dakota city’s tree canopy, which is composed of over 50% ash trees, including those found in local parks and the cemetery. On 21st April 2026, city administrator Steve Pendergrass outlined the financial implications of the crisis, indicating that the cost of removing the trees could reach up to $4 million.Conversely, Milbank's strategy to develop what it describes as 'herd immunity' against the beetle will require an estimated $2.4 million over the next 12 years. This approach aims to enhance the resilience of the ash trees through targeted treatment and monitoring, allowing the city to preserve its green spaces while combating the invasive pest. The strategy is seen as a viable model that could be adapted by other cities in South Dakota facing similar threats to their tree populations.The emerald ash borer, a woodboring beetle, has wreaked havoc across various regions of the United States, leading to widespread tree loss. Milbank's initiative to promote herd immunity reflects an understanding of the ecological importance of maintaining a robust tree canopy, which provides numerous environmental benefits including improved air quality, water retention, and habitat for wildlife. By investing in the health of these trees, Milbank hopes to create a sustainable urban forest that can withstand the challenges posed by invasive species.As Milbank embarks on this ambitious project, the collaboration between local officials, environmental experts, and community members will be crucial. The city plans to implement a series of treatments on a phased basis, focusing on the most vulnerable areas first. This careful approach not only aims to save the existing trees but also hopes to foster a greater awareness of the importance of tree health and biodiversity in urban environments.The successful execution of Milbank's strategy could serve as a template for other communities grappling with similar challenges. As the city takes steps to protect its ash trees, it stands as a testament to the importance of proactive environmental stewardship, offering a beacon of hope for cities that wish to maintain their natural heritage in the face of invasive threats.

urn:contentItem:6JH0-J3Y3-SD88-C45V-00000-00Renewable Energy Update 5.1.262026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisRenewable Energy Update 5.1.26

Allen Matkins Barry Epstein Dana Palmer Tara Paul

May 4th, 2026 ( JD Supra - Delivered by Newstex )

Focus

Solar Power World - April 28

The California Energy Commission has approved the Soda Mountain Solar Project through its Opt-In Certification program. The project is the second to be approved by the California Energy Commission in the program's history. Developed by VC Renewables, the project will be located on land administered by the federal Bureau of Land Management (BLM) northeast of Barstow, California and is contingent on BLM approval. Once built, the project will generate up to 300 MW of renewable energy and include up to 300 MW of battery storage capable of storing 1,200 MWh of energy.

News

Los Angeles Times - April 27

The U.S. Department of the Interior is paying $885 million to two energy companies to abandon offshore wind leases, including a California project off Morro Bay developed by Golden State Wind. The company will receive $120 million in lease fees if it invests equally in oil and gas projects.

PV-Tech - April 27

The New York State Energy Research and Development Authority has launched a new solicitation to procure renewable energy certificates through a competitive process, supporting the development of onshore wind, solar, and hydroelectric projects. The solicitation targets mid-to-late-stage developments that are ready to begin construction, particularly those positioned to benefit from expiring federal clean energy tax credits.

Utility Dive - April 27

A new bill proposed by four Republican members of the U.S. House of Representatives seeks to restore clean tax credits for wind, solar, and other clean energy technologies. The American Energy Dominance Act would remove the accelerated deadlines that the One Big Beautiful Bill Act placed on the renewable energy 45Y production tax credit and 48E investment tax credit, and make similar changes to other impacted credits, like the 45V clean hydrogen production credit.

Projects

KGET - April 24

The Kern County Planning Commission has approved the Discovery Solar Storage Project proposed by Terra-Gen located in the southeast region of the county. The proposed project would generate about 1,400 MW of renewable electrical energy and features 1 GW of energy storage.

Reuters - April 29

Graphic Packaging Holding has signed an agreement with NextEra Energy Resources to build a 250 MW solar power plant in Texas. The deal is Graphic Packaging's largest virtual power purchase agreement to date and forms part of its target to reach net zero emissions by 2050.

Renewables Now - April 29

Massachusetts' governor has announced the activation of the utilities' contracts for the 806 MW Vineyard Wind offshore wind project off the state's coast. The project, which has been producing power since January 2024, has also created nearly 4,000 jobs.

value.Title: DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - THE RECENT SECTOR UPDATES WITH PEERS

urn:contentItem:6JJN-SVX3-S0MR-H4B7-00000-00DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - THE RECENT SECTOR UPDATES WITH PEERS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:00ZLexisNexisSTOCKDAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - THE RECENT SECTOR UPDATES WITH PEERS

DASHBOARD: DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP

PermID4296380340Websitehttp://www.davispolk.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress450 Lexington Avenue New York, NY 10017 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Davis Polk & Wardwell LLP, founded in 1849, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 1,000 lawyers across 10 offices, it serves clients in finance, technology, and healthcare, specializing in M&A, capital markets, and regulatory matters. In 2020, it advised on Morgan Stanleys $13 billion E*Trade acquisition and Gilead Sciences $21 billion Immunomedics deal. In 2024, it expanded its Menlo Park office with key hires like Paul Scrivano from Ropes & Gray. Generating over $1.5 billion annually, its culture emphasizes collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work. Competing with Cravath, Davis Polk stands out for its dealmaking prowess, ranking second in 2020 M&A league tables. Recent initiatives include AI-driven legal tech and ESG advisory services.

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIGENERGY'S HK IPO GAINS MOMENTUM WITH DAVIS POLK'S LEGAL EXPERTISE

Sigenergy Technology, a Chinese energy storage systems maker, has successfully navigated its initial public offering (IPO) in Hong Kong, raising HK$4.4 billion, equivalent to approximately $562 million. The transaction was facilitated by prominent law firms Davis Polk & Wardwell and Jia Yuan Law Offices, who provided critical legal advice throughout the process. This significant event was recorded on the 17th of April 2026.Founded in May 2022, Sigenergy specializes in distributed energy storage systems (DESS) and is headquartered in Shanghai. The company focuses on delivering innovative stackable, distributed solar-plus-storage all-in-one solutions, positioning itself strategically within the rapidly growing energy sector. Sigenergy has emerged as a leader in the market, commanding a 28.6 percent share in the global segment of stackable all-in-one distributed energy storage solutions, according to market data.The IPO was underwritten by a consortium of financial institutions, including CITIC Securities and BNP Paribas, with China International Capital Corporation (CICC) also playing a role as a joint sponsor. Legal support for the underwriters was provided by Linklaters and Jingtian & Gongcheng, with Linklaters’ team led by partner Christine Xu, who oversees Greater China ECM, and capital markets partner Lipton Li, alongside counsel Queenie Tong.In a related development, Mumbai-based Mindspace Business Parks REIT has made headlines with its acquisition of the International Tech Park in Chennai, valued at 30 billion rupees, or $321 million. This transaction was advised by Khaitan & Co, marking a significant investment in the Indian real estate sector. Furthermore, Indian asset manager SBI Funds Management is preparing for a substantial 130-billion-rupee IPO, with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas and Linklaters advising on the endeavor.The successful IPO of Sigenergy Technology not only highlights the company’s robust market position but also reflects the growing investor interest in renewable energy solutions. As the demand for energy storage systems continues to rise, Sigenergy is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the sector.

+ DAVIS POLK STRENGTHENS WEST COAST PRESENCE WITH LA OFFICE LAUNCH

Davis Polk & Wardwell is set to establish a new office in Los Angeles, tapping into the expertise of Jason Russell, the head of Skadden Arps Slate Meagher & Flom's LA office, to lead this expansion. The firm announced this strategic move on 14th April 2026, reinforcing its commitment to grow its influence in key markets.This expansion represents a significant step for Davis Polk, a firm renowned for its legal excellence. By bringing onboard Russell, a seasoned litigator with a robust portfolio of high-profile clients including the NBA, Christie's, and MGA Entertainment, the firm aims to solidify its footing in the lucrative Los Angeles legal landscape.Jason Russell, who has also served on Skadden's policy committee, is expected to play a pivotal role in establishing Davis Polk's presence in LA. His leadership is anticipated to attract additional talent as the firm plans to make further hires for the new office, enhancing its capabilities in the region.Davis Polk's move into Los Angeles not only showcases its ambition to broaden its geographic footprint but also highlights its strategy to compete with other major law firms in California. The firm's focus on high-profile cases and clients could translate into significant opportunities as it seeks to capitalize on the vibrant legal market.As Davis Polk prepares for its Los Angeles office's opening, the legal community will be watching closely. The firm's ability to leverage Russell's extensive experience and connections may position it favorably among clients seeking top-tier legal representation in one of the nation’s most competitive markets.

+ DAVIS POLK'S LIGHTYEAR CAPITAL SECURES $2.5B FOR SIXTH FUND

Lightyear Capital, a private equity firm co-founded by Davis Polk & Wardwell, has successfully raised $2.5 billion for its sixth fund. This significant capital infusion highlights the firm's robust position in the asset management space and reflects investor confidence in its investment strategy and market outlook. The announcement was made public on April 14, 2026.The latest fund is poised to target investments in the financial services and technology sectors, which have shown resilience and growth potential in recent years. With this capital, Lightyear Capital aims to capitalize on emerging opportunities within these industries, leveraging its extensive network and expertise to identify lucrative investment avenues. Davis Polk's longstanding experience in providing legal services to financial institutions further strengthens Lightyear's operational capabilities.The fundraising comes amid a competitive landscape for private equity firms, where securing investor commitments has become increasingly challenging. Lightyear Capital's ability to attract significant backing can be attributed to its track record of strong returns and strategic investment decisions. The firm plans to focus on both established companies and innovative startups, providing them with the necessary support to scale their operations effectively.Davis Polk & Wardwell's involvement in Lightyear Capital is not merely advisory; the firm's attorneys have been integral in structuring the fund and navigating the regulatory landscape. Their expertise ensures that Lightyear operates within legal parameters while maximizing investor returns, a critical factor for stakeholders in today’s complex financial environment. The collaboration between the two entities underscores the synergy between legal prowess and investment acumen.Looking ahead, investors will be keen to see how Lightyear Capital deploys its newly raised capital and whether it can maintain its momentum in generating returns. As the market evolves, the firm’s strategies will be closely scrutinized, particularly in the context of economic fluctuations and sector-specific challenges. With Davis Polk's backing, Lightyear Capital is well-positioned to make a significant impact in the private equity arena.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES UNPRECEDENTED REVENUE GROWTH IN LONDON

Debevoise & Plimpton has reported a remarkable revenue surge of 40% in its London office, reaching a record $268 million. This significant increase has drawn attention in the legal industry and among investors seeking insights into the firm's growth trajectory. The results, announced on 7th April 2026, highlight the firm's robust performance in a competitive market.The leap in revenue is attributed to an uptick in demand for legal services across various sectors, including corporate law and dispute resolution. Debevoise & Plimpton's strategic focus on high-value client engagements and its ability to adapt to changing market conditions have played a key role in this growth. The firm has consistently positioned itself as a leader in providing comprehensive legal solutions to its clientele.In light of this impressive performance, analysts are keenly observing how Debevoise & Plimpton plans to sustain this momentum moving forward. The firm has expressed its commitment to investing in talent and expanding its service offerings, which may further bolster its market position. This proactive approach could potentially attract more clients and drive additional revenue growth in the coming years.The legal sector has witnessed a notable transformation in recent years, and firms like Debevoise & Plimpton that embrace innovation and adapt to new challenges may be well-positioned for continued success. As competition intensifies, maintaining a strong financial performance will be crucial for the firm to retain its competitive edge.As Debevoise & Plimpton celebrates this milestone, the legal community is left wondering whether this growth trend will continue. Investors and clients alike will be looking for signs of sustained performance, as the firm navigates the complexities of the ever-evolving legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES LANDMARK VICTORY FOR YPF S.A. IN $16 BILLION CASE

In a significant legal victory, Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has successfully secured an affirmance from the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit regarding its summary judgment win on behalf of YPF S.A., the largest energy producer in Argentina. This decision, made on 30th March 2026, effectively dismisses claims for over $16 billion in damages and interest, marking a pivotal moment in a case that has lingered in the Southern District ofNew York since 2015.The Second Circuit upheld the District Court’s finding that YPF did not have any contractual obligations to Petersen Energ a Inversora, S.A.U., Petersen Energ a, S.A.U., or Eton Park Capital Management, L.P., along with its associated funds. This ruling is seen as a major victory for YPF, affirming its non-liability for the claims advanced by the plaintiffs, who also included litigation funder Burford Capital.The case traces its roots back to Argentina's 2012 intervention and subsequent expropriation of 51% of YPF's capital stock. The litigation commenced in 2015, and Debevoise took over YPF's defense in December 2020 after earlier motions to dismiss were denied. Following two years of extensive discovery involving numerous expert testimonies, both the plaintiffs and YPF, alongside the Republic of Argentina, sought summary judgment in 2022.In 2023, the District Court granted YPF’s motion for summary judgment in its entirety, adopting Debevoise's arguments that YPF was not obliged under its bylaws to compel the Republic to conduct a tender offer or restrict the Republic's corporate governance rights over the expropriated shares. Conversely, the District Court largely granted the plaintiffs’ motion for summary judgment against the Republic, resulting in a judgment exceeding $16 billion against it in September 2023.Following appeals from all parties involved, the case was fully briefed and argued before the Second Circuit in October 2025. The affirmation of YPF's victory not only reinforces the firm's position but also highlights the complexities involved in international energy disputes and expropriation cases, setting a notable precedent in the legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE ELEVATES JEFFREY SULLIVAN KC AND INA POPOVA TO EUROPEAN DISPUTES LEADERSHIP

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has announced the appointment of partners Jeffrey Sullivan KC and Ina Popova as Co-Chairs of European Disputes, effective immediately. This strategic move enhances the firm's presence in key markets, particularly in Paris, Frankfurt, and Luxembourg. The announcement comes on the 17th of March, 2026, highlighting the firm's commitment to strengthening its capabilities in international dispute resolution across mainland Europe.Catherine Amirfar, who serves as Co-Chair of Debevoise's global International Dispute Resolution Group, expressed confidence in the leadership of Sullivan KC and Popova. She stated, 'Ina and Jeff’s leadership will build on Debevoise’s long-standing strength in major disputes across mainland Europe, ensuring we continue to deliver the highest level of advocacy and strategic counsel to our clients.' Their appointments align with the firm's strategy of appointing regional leaders to drive its dispute resolution practice worldwide, joining other prominent leaders in Latin America and Asia.Jeffrey Sullivan KC is already recognized as a leading figure in international arbitration and public international law. In his new role, he will continue to focus on high-stakes commercial and investor-state arbitrations, representing a diverse clientele that includes companies and private equity sponsors. His established reputation in the market is complemented by his experience as an arbitrator in significant cases.Ina Popova brings a wealth of experience in international arbitration and public international law, focusing on managing and litigating complex disputes. Her dual qualification in civil and common law, along with her fluency in multiple languages, positions her uniquely to navigate the intricacies of cross-border disputes. Popova's previous role as a member of the ICC International Court of Arbitration further underscores her expertise and credibility in the field.With over four decades of experience, Debevoise’s International Dispute Resolution Group has consistently delivered exceptional outcomes in international arbitration and public international law. The team's extensive expertise spans multiple jurisdictions and languages, making it a formidable player in the global disputes arena. Recent engagements have involved over 125 countries and stakes exceeding $110 billion, showcasing Debevoise's commitment to achieving significant results for its clients.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [2 News That Matters]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL'S RODGE COHEN REFLECTS ON FIVE DECADES IN BANKING

H. Rodgin “Rodge” Cohen, senior chair at Sullivan & Cromwell, has been a transformative force in the banking industry for over five decades. On 19th February 2026, during an event hosted by NYU Law's Institute for Corporate Governance & Finance (ICGF), Cohen shared his experiences and insights on the evolution of the modern financial system. The conversation was enriched by contributions from Jay Clayton, former chairman of the US Securities and Exchange Commission, and Edward Rock, co-director of ICGF.Cohen's legal career has coincided with numerous landmark events that have shaped the financial landscape. Clayton, who has previously worked alongside Cohen at Sullivan & Cromwell, highlighted the profound changes in banking regulation since the 1970s. Cohen emphasized the significance of the Bank Secrecy Act and the Community Reinvestment Act, noting that these measures have drastically changed access to banking services, reducing the percentage of unbanked Americans from one-third to just five percent.A defining moment in Cohen's career came during the 2008 global financial crisis, where he represented several major banks in efforts to stabilize the financial system. Reflecting on the crisis, Cohen recalled the critical hours leading up to the government’s decision to intervene after the collapse of Lehman Brothers. He stated, 'We were on the edge of a cliff,' emphasizing the urgency and gravity of the situation. His efforts during this tumultuous time solidified his reputation as a leading financial services lawyer.Cohen's influence extends beyond crisis management; he has also played a crucial role in regulatory changes that have transformed the banking industry. For instance, his advocacy for interstate banking in the 1980s allowed banks to expand their operations across state lines, significantly reshaping the banking landscape. Clayton praised Cohen’s ability to interpret statutes creatively, which has been vital for navigating the complexities of financial regulation.As the conversation turned to future challenges, Cohen highlighted the importance of effective cryptocurrency regulation. He warned of potential pitfalls if regulations do not keep pace with the evolving market, likening the situation to Gresham’s Law. 'Bad regulation drives out good regulation,' he cautioned, stressing the need for robust frameworks to manage the risks associated with cryptocurrency. Cohen's insights serve as a reminder of the ongoing evolution of the financial sector and the critical role of firms like Sullivan & Cromwell in shaping its future.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL EXPANDS LONDON FINANCE TEAM WITH NEW PARTNERS

Sullivan & Cromwell LLP has announced the addition of Christopher McLaughlin and Alastair McVeigh as partners in its London office. This strategic move underscores the firm’s commitment to bolstering its private capital capabilities in the UK and European markets. The announcement follows the date of 21st January 2026, marking a significant step in the firm’s expansion plans.Christopher McLaughlin will spearhead the firm's private equity sponsor and borrower acquisition, as well as leveraged finance practices in London. Meanwhile, Alastair McVeigh will lead the European private credit offering, focusing on direct and specialty lending. The deal was officially announced on 24th March 2026, with Wachtell Lipton advising its long-standing client, Public Storage, the largest self-storage company in the United States by market capitalization, on its acquisition of National Storage Affiliates.Clifford Chance is representing National Storage Affiliates in this significant all-stock transaction, which will boost Public Storage's market capitalization to approximately $57 billion. The acquisition will enable the combined entity to oversee an impressive square footage equivalent to that of a small city, expanding its footprint significantly in key U.S. markets.Leading Wachtell Lipton's team is Adam Emmerich, co-chair of the corporate department, alongside M&A partner Kyle Diamond. Emmerich has a history of advising Public Storage on major transactions, including its $6 billion acquisition of Shurgard in 2006 and the $2.2 billion purchase of Simply Self Storage from Blackstone Real Estate Income Trust in 2023. The firm’s expertise is expected to play a crucial role in navigating the complexities of this latest acquisition.Clifford Chance's team is spearheaded by M&A partners Chang-Do Gong and Robert Chung, who have both recently joined the firm from White & Case. They are joined by capital markets partner Andrew Epstein and real estate partner Jeanne Roig-Irwin. Meanwhile, DLA Piper is providing real estate financing counsel to Public Storage, with its team led by partner Amy Carbins and supported by New York-based partner Viktor Sapezhnikov.Under the terms of the agreement, shareholders of National Storage will receive 0.14 of a share of Public Storage common stock for each National Storage share they own, translating to a total consideration of $41.68 per share. The transaction is anticipated to close in the third quarter of 2026, following the formation of a joint venture that will encompass 313 properties with an estimated value of approximately $3.3 billion.

+ ELON MUSK'S X CORP ENDS $90M LEGAL BATTLE WITH WACHTELL LIPTON

In a swift legal pivot, Elon Musk’s X Corp has voluntarily dismissed its high-stakes $90 million lawsuit against powerhouse law firm Wachtell Lipton Rosen & Katz, closing a bitter chapter from the chaotic 2022 Twitter takeover. The lawsuit, filed in San FranciscoSuperior Court back on 1st October 2023, accused Wachtell of pocketing an 'improper bonus payment' that violated fiduciary duties, claiming Twitter executives inflated fees with 'success' bonuses just before the $44 billion deal closed.Wachtell Lipton, which represented the old Twitter board in the fierce battle to compel Musk to honor his buyout pledge, responded to the accusations with vigor. The firm emphasized its 'complete success' after months of litigation, which ultimately secured billions for shareholders. They asserted that the fees in question were approved by the board itself, effectively countering the claims made by X Corp.The dismissal of the lawsuit, which was revealed in a court filing reported by Reuters, bars refiling and indicates a clean break between the two parties. A spokesperson for Wachtell Lipton expressed satisfaction with the outcome, stating, 'We are pleased that X has dismissed its meritless lawsuit with prejudice. There was no settlement.' This marks a significant turn in the ongoing legal saga surrounding the Twitter acquisition.This legal retreat comes on the heels of another reconciliation for X Corp, which recently acknowledged a $128 million executive payout suit related to the tumultuous period during the Twitter acquisition. The dismissal signals a potential shift in strategy for X Corp, as it seeks to distance itself from the financial and legal entanglements of its predecessor.As the legal landscape continues to evolve for Elon Musk's X Corp, the resolution with Wachtell Lipton may pave the way for a more focused approach to its business operations. Investors and market observers will be keenly watching how these developments influence the company's trajectory moving forward.
