urn:contentItem:6JG9-Y673-SJ9G-V112-00000-00DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - RECENT NEWS OF DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP AND PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:00ZLexisNexisSTOCKDAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - RECENT NEWS OF DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP

PermID4296380340Websitehttp://www.davispolk.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress450 Lexington Avenue New York, NY 10017 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Davis Polk & Wardwell LLP, founded in 1849, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 1,000 lawyers across 10 offices, it serves clients in finance, technology, and healthcare, specializing in M&A, capital markets, and regulatory matters. In 2020, it advised on Morgan Stanleys $13 billion E*Trade acquisition and Gilead Sciences $21 billion Immunomedics deal. In 2024, it expanded its Menlo Park office with key hires like Paul Scrivano from Ropes & Gray. Generating over $1.5 billion annually, its culture emphasizes collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work. Competing with Cravath, Davis Polk stands out for its dealmaking prowess, ranking second in 2020 M&A league tables. Recent initiatives include AI-driven legal tech and ESG advisory services.

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIGENERGY'S HK IPO GAINS MOMENTUM WITH DAVIS POLK'S LEGAL EXPERTISE

Sigenergy Technology, a Chinese energy storage systems maker, has successfully navigated its initial public offering (IPO) in Hong Kong, raising HK$4.4 billion, equivalent to approximately $562 million. The transaction was facilitated by prominent law firms Davis Polk & Wardwell and Jia Yuan Law Offices, who provided critical legal advice throughout the process. This significant event was recorded on the 17th of April 2026.Founded in May 2022, Sigenergy specializes in distributed energy storage systems (DESS) and is headquartered in Shanghai. The company focuses on delivering innovative stackable, distributed solar-plus-storage all-in-one solutions, positioning itself strategically within the rapidly growing energy sector. Sigenergy has emerged as a leader in the market, commanding a 28.6 percent share in the global segment of stackable all-in-one distributed energy storage solutions, according to market data.The IPO was underwritten by a consortium of financial institutions, including CITIC Securities and BNP Paribas, with China International Capital Corporation (CICC) also playing a role as a joint sponsor. Legal support for the underwriters was provided by Linklaters and Jingtian & Gongcheng, with Linklaters’ team led by partner Christine Xu, who oversees Greater China ECM, and capital markets partner Lipton Li, alongside counsel Queenie Tong.In a related development, Mumbai-based Mindspace Business Parks REIT has made headlines with its acquisition of the International Tech Park in Chennai, valued at 30 billion rupees, or $321 million. This transaction was advised by Khaitan & Co, marking a significant investment in the Indian real estate sector. Furthermore, Indian asset manager SBI Funds Management is preparing for a substantial 130-billion-rupee IPO, with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas and Linklaters advising on the endeavor.The successful IPO of Sigenergy Technology not only highlights the company’s robust market position but also reflects the growing investor interest in renewable energy solutions. As the demand for energy storage systems continues to rise, Sigenergy is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the sector.

+ DAVIS POLK'S LIGHTYEAR CAPITAL SECURES $2.5B FOR SIXTH FUND

Lightyear Capital, a private equity firm co-founded by Davis Polk & Wardwell, has successfully raised $2.5 billion for its sixth fund. This significant capital infusion highlights the firm's robust position in the asset management space and reflects investor confidence in its investment strategy and market outlook. The announcement was made public on April 14, 2026.The latest fund is poised to target investments in the financial services and technology sectors, which have shown resilience and growth potential in recent years. With this capital, Lightyear Capital aims to capitalize on emerging opportunities within these industries, leveraging its extensive network and expertise to identify lucrative investment avenues. Davis Polk's longstanding experience in providing legal services to financial institutions further strengthens Lightyear's operational capabilities.The fundraising comes amid a competitive landscape for private equity firms, where securing investor commitments has become increasingly challenging. Lightyear Capital's ability to attract significant backing can be attributed to its track record of strong returns and strategic investment decisions. The firm plans to focus on both established companies and innovative startups, providing them with the necessary support to scale their operations effectively.Davis Polk & Wardwell's involvement in Lightyear Capital is not merely advisory; the firm's attorneys have been integral in structuring the fund and navigating the regulatory landscape. Their expertise ensures that Lightyear operates within legal parameters while maximizing investor returns, a critical factor for stakeholders in today’s complex financial environment. The collaboration between the two entities underscores the synergy between legal prowess and investment acumen.Looking ahead, investors will be keen to see how Lightyear Capital deploys its newly raised capital and whether it can maintain its momentum in generating returns. As the market evolves, the firm’s strategies will be closely scrutinized, particularly in the context of economic fluctuations and sector-specific challenges. With Davis Polk's backing, Lightyear Capital is well-positioned to make a significant impact in the private equity arena.

+ DAVIS POLK STRENGTHENS WEST COAST PRESENCE WITH LA OFFICE LAUNCH

Davis Polk & Wardwell is set to establish a new office in Los Angeles, tapping into the expertise of Jason Russell, the head of Skadden Arps Slate Meagher & Flom's LA office, to lead this expansion. The firm announced this strategic move on 14th April 2026, reinforcing its commitment to grow its influence in key markets.This expansion represents a significant step for Davis Polk, a firm renowned for its legal excellence. By bringing onboard Russell, a seasoned litigator with a robust portfolio of high-profile clients including the NBA, Christie's, and MGA Entertainment, the firm aims to solidify its footing in the lucrative Los Angeles legal landscape.Jason Russell, who has also served on Skadden's policy committee, is expected to play a pivotal role in establishing Davis Polk's presence in LA. His leadership is anticipated to attract additional talent as the firm plans to make further hires for the new office, enhancing its capabilities in the region.Davis Polk's move into Los Angeles not only showcases its ambition to broaden its geographic footprint but also highlights its strategy to compete with other major law firms in California. The firm's focus on high-profile cases and clients could translate into significant opportunities as it seeks to capitalize on the vibrant legal market.As Davis Polk prepares for its Los Angeles office's opening, the legal community will be watching closely. The firm's ability to leverage Russell's extensive experience and connections may position it favorably among clients seeking top-tier legal representation in one of the nation’s most competitive markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES UNPRECEDENTED REVENUE GROWTH IN LONDON

Debevoise & Plimpton has reported a remarkable revenue surge of 40% in its London office, reaching a record $268 million. This significant increase has drawn attention in the legal industry and among investors seeking insights into the firm's growth trajectory. The results, announced on 7th April 2026, highlight the firm's robust performance in a competitive market.The leap in revenue is attributed to an uptick in demand for legal services across various sectors, including corporate law and dispute resolution. Debevoise & Plimpton's strategic focus on high-value client engagements and its ability to adapt to changing market conditions have played a key role in this growth. The firm has consistently positioned itself as a leader in providing comprehensive legal solutions to its clientele.In light of this impressive performance, analysts are keenly observing how Debevoise & Plimpton plans to sustain this momentum moving forward. The firm has expressed its commitment to investing in talent and expanding its service offerings, which may further bolster its market position. This proactive approach could potentially attract more clients and drive additional revenue growth in the coming years.The legal sector has witnessed a notable transformation in recent years, and firms like Debevoise & Plimpton that embrace innovation and adapt to new challenges may be well-positioned for continued success. As competition intensifies, maintaining a strong financial performance will be crucial for the firm to retain its competitive edge.As Debevoise & Plimpton celebrates this milestone, the legal community is left wondering whether this growth trend will continue. Investors and clients alike will be looking for signs of sustained performance, as the firm navigates the complexities of the ever-evolving legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON ACHIEVES LANDMARK VICTORY FOR YPF S.A. IN $16 BILLION CASE

In a significant legal victory, Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has successfully secured an affirmance from the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit regarding its summary judgment win on behalf of YPF S.A., the largest energy producer in Argentina. This decision, made on 30th March 2026, effectively dismisses claims for over $16 billion in damages and interest, marking a pivotal moment in a case that has lingered in the Southern District ofNew York since 2015.The Second Circuit upheld the District Court’s finding that YPF did not have any contractual obligations to Petersen Energ a Inversora, S.A.U., Petersen Energ a, S.A.U., or Eton Park Capital Management, L.P., along with its associated funds. This ruling is seen as a major victory for YPF, affirming its non-liability for the claims advanced by the plaintiffs, who also included litigation funder Burford Capital.The case traces its roots back to Argentina's 2012 intervention and subsequent expropriation of 51% of YPF's capital stock. The litigation commenced in 2015, and Debevoise took over YPF's defense in December 2020 after earlier motions to dismiss were denied. Following two years of extensive discovery involving numerous expert testimonies, both the plaintiffs and YPF, alongside the Republic of Argentina, sought summary judgment in 2022.In 2023, the District Court granted YPF’s motion for summary judgment in its entirety, adopting Debevoise's arguments that YPF was not obliged under its bylaws to compel the Republic to conduct a tender offer or restrict the Republic's corporate governance rights over the expropriated shares. Conversely, the District Court largely granted the plaintiffs’ motion for summary judgment against the Republic, resulting in a judgment exceeding $16 billion against it in September 2023.Following appeals from all parties involved, the case was fully briefed and argued before the Second Circuit in October 2025. The affirmation of YPF's victory not only reinforces the firm's position but also highlights the complexities involved in international energy disputes and expropriation cases, setting a notable precedent in the legal landscape.

+ DEBEVOISE ELEVATES JEFFREY SULLIVAN KC AND INA POPOVA TO EUROPEAN DISPUTES LEADERSHIP

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has announced the appointment of partners Jeffrey Sullivan KC and Ina Popova as Co-Chairs of European Disputes, effective immediately. This strategic move enhances the firm's presence in key markets, particularly in Paris, Frankfurt, and Luxembourg. The announcement comes on the 17th of March, 2026, highlighting the firm's commitment to strengthening its capabilities in international dispute resolution across mainland Europe.Catherine Amirfar, who serves as Co-Chair of Debevoise's global International Dispute Resolution Group, expressed confidence in the leadership of Sullivan KC and Popova. She stated, 'Ina and Jeff’s leadership will build on Debevoise’s long-standing strength in major disputes across mainland Europe, ensuring we continue to deliver the highest level of advocacy and strategic counsel to our clients.' Their appointments align with the firm's strategy of appointing regional leaders to drive its dispute resolution practice worldwide, joining other prominent leaders in Latin America and Asia.Jeffrey Sullivan KC is already recognized as a leading figure in international arbitration and public international law. In his new role, he will continue to focus on high-stakes commercial and investor-state arbitrations, representing a diverse clientele that includes companies and private equity sponsors. His established reputation in the market is complemented by his experience as an arbitrator in significant cases.Ina Popova brings a wealth of experience in international arbitration and public international law, focusing on managing and litigating complex disputes. Her dual qualification in civil and common law, along with her fluency in multiple languages, positions her uniquely to navigate the intricacies of cross-border disputes. Popova's previous role as a member of the ICC International Court of Arbitration further underscores her expertise and credibility in the field.With over four decades of experience, Debevoise’s International Dispute Resolution Group has consistently delivered exceptional outcomes in international arbitration and public international law. The team's extensive expertise spans multiple jurisdictions and languages, making it a formidable player in the global disputes arena. Recent engagements have involved over 125 countries and stakes exceeding $110 billion, showcasing Debevoise's commitment to achieving significant results for its clients.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [2 News That Matters]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL'S RODGE COHEN REFLECTS ON FIVE DECADES IN BANKING

H. Rodgin “Rodge” Cohen, senior chair at Sullivan & Cromwell, has been a transformative force in the banking industry for over five decades. On 19th February 2026, during an event hosted by NYU Law's Institute for Corporate Governance & Finance (ICGF), Cohen shared his experiences and insights on the evolution of the modern financial system. The conversation was enriched by contributions from Jay Clayton, former chairman of the US Securities and Exchange Commission, and Edward Rock, co-director of ICGF.Cohen's legal career has coincided with numerous landmark events that have shaped the financial landscape. Clayton, who has previously worked alongside Cohen at Sullivan & Cromwell, highlighted the profound changes in banking regulation since the 1970s. Cohen emphasized the significance of the Bank Secrecy Act and the Community Reinvestment Act, noting that these measures have drastically changed access to banking services, reducing the percentage of unbanked Americans from one-third to just five percent.A defining moment in Cohen's career came during the 2008 global financial crisis, where he represented several major banks in efforts to stabilize the financial system. Reflecting on the crisis, Cohen recalled the critical hours leading up to the government’s decision to intervene after the collapse of Lehman Brothers. He stated, 'We were on the edge of a cliff,' emphasizing the urgency and gravity of the situation. His efforts during this tumultuous time solidified his reputation as a leading financial services lawyer.Cohen's influence extends beyond crisis management; he has also played a crucial role in regulatory changes that have transformed the banking industry. For instance, his advocacy for interstate banking in the 1980s allowed banks to expand their operations across state lines, significantly reshaping the banking landscape. Clayton praised Cohen’s ability to interpret statutes creatively, which has been vital for navigating the complexities of financial regulation.As the conversation turned to future challenges, Cohen highlighted the importance of effective cryptocurrency regulation. He warned of potential pitfalls if regulations do not keep pace with the evolving market, likening the situation to Gresham’s Law. 'Bad regulation drives out good regulation,' he cautioned, stressing the need for robust frameworks to manage the risks associated with cryptocurrency. Cohen's insights serve as a reminder of the ongoing evolution of the financial sector and the critical role of firms like Sullivan & Cromwell in shaping its future.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL EXPANDS LONDON FINANCE TEAM WITH NEW PARTNERS

Sullivan & Cromwell LLP has announced the addition of Christopher McLaughlin and Alastair McVeigh as partners in its London office. This strategic move underscores the firm’s commitment to bolstering its private capital capabilities in the UK and European markets. The announcement follows the date of 21st January 2026, marking a significant step in the firm’s expansion plans.Christopher McLaughlin will spearhead the firm's private equity sponsor and borrower acquisition, as well as leveraged finance practices in London. Meanwhile, Alastair McVeigh will lead the European private credit offering, focusing on direct and specialty lending. Robert Giuffra and Scott Miller, Co-Chairs of Sullivan & Cromwell, expressed enthusiasm for the new hires, noting that their expertise will enhance the firm's ability to serve private equity sponsors, lenders, and private credit providers across the region.McLaughlin is recognized as a leading figure in acquisition and leveraged finance law within Europe, boasting extensive experience in advising private equity sponsors and private credit providers. His accolades include being ranked as a Leading Individual for Leveraged Finance by Chambers UK and a Leading Partner for Acquisition Finance by Legal 500. Similarly, McVeigh is well-respected for his work in leveraged finance, particularly in private credit and specialty lending, positioning him as a valuable asset to Sullivan & Cromwell’s growing practice.The co-managing partners of Sullivan & Cromwell's London office, Kon Asimacopoulos and John Horsfield-Bradbury, highlighted that the arrivals of McLaughlin and McVeigh are integral to the execution of the firm's private capital strategy. They emphasized that the new partners’ top-tier expertise aligns seamlessly with the firm’s global platform and its established finance practice in the U.S.Sullivan & Cromwell's Private Equity Practice is known for representing top financial sponsors and private capital investors in complex transactions globally. With an average deal size of $2.3 billion for completed European private equity transactions in 2024, according to Mergermarket, the firm’s reputation for excellence continues to grow. The addition of McLaughlin and McVeigh is expected to further enhance the firm’s capabilities across the spectrum of private credit and leveraged finance, solidifying its position as a leader in these sectors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB [3 News That Matters]

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB STRENGTHENS DIGITAL ASSETS GROUP WITH IGOR KLEYMAN

Cleary Gottlieb announced the reappointment of Igor Kleyman as a partner in its financial institutions practice, focusing on digital assets, artificial intelligence (AI), and fintech. This announcement was made on 23rd April 2026, highlighting the firm's commitment to enhancing its capabilities in rapidly evolving sectors.Igor Kleyman rejoins Cleary Gottlieb after nearly a decade of experience advising clients on critical issues surrounding digital assets and AI. Most recently, he served as Head of Responsible AI Governance at Amazon, and prior to that, he held key legal positions at Nasdaq and Gemini Trust Company, where he navigated regulatory challenges and contributed to the development of pioneering financial products.Managing Partner Jeff Karpf expressed enthusiasm about Igor's return, stating, "Igor’s fluency across the transformational fintech and AI sectors, combined with his sophisticated understanding of financial regulatory structures and institutions, enhances our powerhouse of innovation in financial services." This sentiment underscores the firm's strategy to remain at the forefront of legal services in these dynamic fields.Igor will be part of Cleary Gottlieb's multidisciplinary digital assets group, which was recognized as a 2025 Law360 Practice Group of the Year. The group is noted for its efforts in shaping regulatory frameworks for fintech and managing significant enforcement actions for its digital asset clients. Igor's expertise in compliance and governance has played a crucial role in establishing industry standards.In his own words, Igor expressed excitement about his return to Cleary Gottlieb, stating, "I look forward to collaborating across Cleary’s global network to further strengthen our relationships with fintech companies and businesses exploring digital assets, tokenization, and AI offerings." This collaboration aligns with Cleary Gottlieb's longstanding reputation as a leader in digital assets and fintech law.

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB WELCOMES BACK DIGITAL ASSETS AND AI SPECIALIST FROM AMAZON

Cleary Gottlieb has announced the return of a prominent expert in digital assets and artificial intelligence (AI) who previously held a senior position at Amazon. This strategic move reflects the firm's commitment to enhancing its capabilities in the rapidly evolving digital landscape. The professional, whose return is eagerly anticipated within the legal community, had been instrumental in Amazon's initiatives related to AI technologies and digital asset management.On 20th April 2026, the firm confirmed that the digital assets and AI specialist has rejoined its ranks following a successful tenure at Amazon. This hire comes at a time when legal frameworks surrounding digital currencies and AI applications are becoming increasingly complex, necessitating experienced legal guidance. Cleary Gottlieb aims to leverage this expertise to better serve its clients, particularly in financial technology and regulatory compliance.The rejoining of this expert is a notable addition to Cleary Gottlieb's already robust practice in digital assets. The firm has been actively involved in advising clients on blockchain technologies, cryptocurrency regulations, and the implications of AI within the financial sector. By reintegrating this professional, Cleary Gottlieb not only strengthens its position in these specialized areas but also enhances its overall service offerings to clients navigating the intricacies of digital innovation.As the demand for legal services in the tech sector grows, firms like Cleary Gottlieb are increasingly focused on recruiting talent with unique expertise in emerging technologies. The legal landscape is witnessing a significant shift, with traditional practices evolving to meet the needs of clients in various industries, from fintech startups to established corporations looking to innovate. The return of this digital assets and AI expert signals Cleary Gottlieb's proactive approach to stay ahead in this competitive environment.With the legal implications of digital assets and AI continuing to unfold, the expertise of this newly reinstated professional is likely to be a significant asset for Cleary Gottlieb. Clients seeking guidance on navigating the regulatory complexities associated with these technologies will benefit from the firm's enhanced knowledge base. As Cleary Gottlieb positions itself at the forefront of the digital transformation, this hire may also spark increased interest and investment from clients eager to explore new opportunities in the digital economy.

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB UNVEILS CX+, AN AI SOLUTION FOR LEGAL WORKFLOW OPTIMIZATION

Cleary Gottlieb has announced the launch of CX+, a proprietary AI-powered platform designed to enhance legal workflows through advanced contract intelligence and due diligence capabilities. The announcement was made on the 15th of April 2026, and CX+ is comprised of two main products: CX+Insights and CX+Transact, which cater specifically to the operational and transactional needs of in-house legal teams and deal professionals.Developed in collaboration with senior in-house clients and legal operations experts, CX+ was crafted to address specific challenges faced by legal teams. Carla Swansburg, CEO of ClearyX, stated, "We spent years speaking with clients about the problems that they wish technology would solve for them, and that generic tools simply aren't solving." The platform aims to provide tailored solutions that fit seamlessly within the existing technology stacks of clients, enhancing their operational efficiency.CX+ is designed to be adaptable and model-agnostic, allowing clients to configure the platform according to their unique workflows. The software prioritizes security, featuring secure code and rigorous testing for both accuracy and stability. Notably, CX+ can integrate with existing document management systems through custom APIs, enabling immediate value realization from the outset. Clients can choose to license CX+ as a standalone tool or combine it with additional services from ClearyX's expert team.CX+Insights specifically targets in-house legal teams seeking to derive systematic intelligence from their contract portfolios. The platform facilitates the loading of contracts, enabling users to query documents with AI-driven question bundles and generate actionable insights via custom dashboards and visualizations. As new documents are added, CX+Insights automatically updates its analytics, creating a continuous intelligence layer that enhances decision-making processes.CX+Transact, on the other hand, is tailored for contract review and M&A due diligence. It features a comprehensive review system that allows for tagging, filtering, and in-place editing, along with customizable workflows and real-time visibility into review progress. ClearyX has documented substantial cost and time savings of 40-60% on due diligence tasks compared to traditional manual review methods across more than 150 deals. This innovative platform underscores Cleary Gottlieb's commitment to leveraging technology to deliver exceptional client service.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ LATHAM & WATKINS AND LOEB & LOEB LEAD AI BATTERY FIRM IN $250M SPAC DEAL

Latham & Watkins and Loeb & Loeb have taken leading roles in the $250 million merger of AI Battery Company with a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC). This transaction is poised to bolster the AI Battery Company's financial resources and accelerate its growth trajectory in the burgeoning market for artificial intelligence-driven battery technologies. The merger is expected to close shortly, following regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions.The deal, announced on 21st April 2026, marks a significant milestone for AI Battery Company, which is focused on developing advanced battery solutions that leverage artificial intelligence for improved efficiency and sustainability. The merger with the SPAC aims to provide the necessary capital infusion to enhance research and development efforts, as well as to expand the company's manufacturing capabilities.Latham & Watkins has been instrumental in navigating the complex legal landscape associated with SPAC transactions, drawing on its extensive experience in capital markets and mergers and acquisitions. The firm’s involvement underscores its commitment to supporting innovative companies in the technology sector as they seek to access public markets. Loeb & Loeb's expertise complements Latham’s efforts, particularly in regulatory compliance and corporate governance matters.Investors are keenly observing the progress of AI Battery Company's merger, as the demand for AI-integrated solutions continues to rise. The company's approach to battery technology positions it at the forefront of a rapidly evolving industry, where efficiency and sustainability are critical. The SPAC merger not only provides a pathway to public listing but also enhances the company’s visibility within the investment community.As the transaction nears completion, stakeholders are optimistic about the future of AI Battery Company in the context of an increasingly competitive market. The legal guidance from Latham & Watkins and Loeb & Loeb is expected to play a crucial role in ensuring a smooth transition into public ownership, setting the stage for further growth and innovation in the AI battery space.

+ LATHAM & WATKINS ACHIEVES RECORD GLOBAL REVENUE OF $8.3 BILLION

Latham & Watkins LLP has reported record financial results for fiscal year 2025, with global gross revenue reaching $8.3 billion, marking an 18.6% increase from approximately $7 billion in 2024. This announcement comes on the 16th of April, 2026, following the firm’s submissions for the 2026 Am Law 100 rankings released on the same day. Profit per equity partner (PEP) surged 21.3% to approximately $8.7 million, underscoring the firm’s strong financial positioning.A key highlight of Latham’s financial performance is its London office, which crossed the $1 billion revenue threshold for the first time, up from approximately $850 million in 2024, signifying growth of more than 17%. This achievement places Latham alongside Kirkland & Ellis as one of only two major US-headquartered firms to reach this milestone in the UK market. + MILBANK'S STRATEGIC PLAN TO COMBAT INVASIVE BEETLE THREATENS ASH TREES

MILBANK — In a proactive response to an invasive species threat, city leaders in Milbank are implementing a comprehensive strategy to protect their ash trees from the emerald ash borer. The infestation, first detected last summer, poses a significant risk to the northeastern South Dakota city’s tree canopy, which is composed of over 50% ash trees, including those found in local parks and the cemetery. On 21st April 2026, city administrator Steve Pendergrass outlined the financial implications of the crisis, indicating that the cost of removing the trees could reach up to $4 million.Conversely, Milbank's strategy to develop what it describes as 'herd immunity' against the beetle will require an estimated $2.4 million over the next 12 years. This approach aims to enhance the resilience of the ash trees through targeted treatment and monitoring, allowing the city to preserve its green spaces while combating the invasive pest. The strategy is seen as a viable model that could be adapted by other cities in South Dakota facing similar threats to their tree populations.The emerald ash borer, a woodboring beetle, has wreaked havoc across various regions of the United States, leading to widespread tree loss. Milbank's initiative to promote herd immunity reflects an understanding of the ecological importance of maintaining a robust tree canopy, which provides numerous environmental benefits including improved air quality, water retention, and habitat for wildlife. By investing in the health of these trees, Milbank hopes to create a sustainable urban forest that can withstand the challenges posed by invasive species.As Milbank embarks on this ambitious project, the collaboration between local officials, environmental experts, and community members will be crucial. The city plans to implement a series of treatments on a phased basis, focusing on the most vulnerable areas first. This careful approach not only aims to save the existing trees but also hopes to foster a greater awareness of the importance of tree health and biodiversity in urban environments.The successful execution of Milbank's strategy could serve as a template for other communities grappling with similar challenges. As the city takes steps to protect its ash trees, it stands as a testament to the importance of proactive environmental stewardship, offering a beacon of hope for cities that wish to maintain their natural heritage in the face of invasive threats.

+ MILBANK ANALYZES CFTC'S NEW GUIDANCE ON PREDICTION MARKETS

In a recent webinar, Milbank attorneys examined the Commodity Futures Trading Commission’s (CFTC) latest guidance on prediction markets, which was released last week. The event, titled 'The CFTC’s New Guidance on Prediction Markets: What You Need to Know,' took place on Tuesday. This follows the CFTC's announcement on 17th March 2026, introducing a regulatory expectations advisory and an Advanced Notice of Proposed Rulemaking, initiating a 45-day public comment period that concludes on 30th April.Milbank Partner Josh Sterling opened the discussion by highlighting the remarkable growth of prediction markets, which surged from $3 billion in trading volume in January 2025 to $27 billion in January 2026. This growth has attracted attention not only from those aiming to rejuvenate financial markets but also from the courts and state regulators. The session featured Milbank litigation partners Grant Mainland, Matt Laroche, and John Williams, who provided insights on the current state of the prediction market industry and the implications of the CFTC's recent announcements for market operators.Laroche emphasized that the CFTC's advisory indicates the commission's belief that contracts related to sports events fall under its jurisdiction, which he described as a significant clarification. He stated, 'The purpose of the advisory was to provide right up from that the CFTC is recognizing it is a unique financial asset class that is rapidly increasing and provides a reliable source of information for a variety of uses.' This recognition could influence ongoing litigation regarding the classification of such contracts.Williams noted that the notice of rulemaking represents a strong stance by the CFTC to assert jurisdiction over these contracts, though it does not clearly outline future regulatory directions. He remarked on the extensive list of questions posed for public comment, which invites input on how event contracts might contribute to hedging, price risk, and information dissemination. 'It’s a good sign the commission is thinking about those things,' he added, indicating a focus on public interest.To navigate the changing regulatory landscape, Milbank attorneys outlined practical steps for prediction market operators. Recommendations included evaluating existing contract listings for compliance, establishing relationships with sports leagues, assessing manipulation risks, participating in the rulemaking process, and staying informed about blockchain developments. Mainland also provided an overview of over 20 ongoing court cases related to prediction markets, indicating that the legal landscape remains complex and uncertain, especially regarding the question of whether the Commodity Exchange Act preempts state law.

+ MILBANK TO FACILITATE DISCUSSION FOR KENNEDY-WILSON BONDHOLDERS

Milbank LLP will host a call for holders of Kennedy-Wilson, Inc.'s 4.75% senior notes due 2029, 4.75% senior notes due 2030, and 5.00% senior notes due 2031. The call is set to take place today, 5th March 2026, at 3:30 p.m. (EST), addressing concerns raised by numerous bondholders regarding the recent Exchange Offer and consent solicitation statement dated 2nd March 2026.This call is particularly significant as Milbank has received indications of interest from holders of a substantial portion of the Notes, suggesting a heightened level of engagement among investors. Bondholders who wish to participate in the call must provide their detailed contact information, along with their holdings information on a series-by-series basis, to ensure their inclusion in the discussion.Milbank LLP, a prominent international law firm founded over 150 years ago in New York, specializes in providing innovative legal services to clients globally. With offices in key financial hubs including London, Hong Kong, and S o Paulo, Milbank collaborates across various practices to assist commercial, financial, and industrial enterprises, as well as institutions and governments, in achieving their strategic objectives.In addition to hosting calls for Kennedy-Wilson bondholders, Milbank has been actively analyzing recent developments affecting unsecured creditors of Braskem S.A. This highlights the firm’s commitment to providing timely and relevant legal advice to its clients in an ever-evolving financial landscape.As Milbank continues to expand its footprint and enhance its services, the firm remains dedicated to fostering transparent communication between issuers and investors, ensuring that concerns are addressed promptly and effectively.

urn:contentItem:6JH6-VP43-RS0S-X4VB-00000-00Create Energy, LLC Trademark Application for "REIMAGINING RENEWABLES" Filed2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisCREATE ENERGY LLCCreate Energy, LLC Trademark Application for "REIMAGINING RENEWABLES" Filed


2026 MAY 05 (NewsRx) -- By a News Reporter-Staff News Editor at Energy Daily News -- According to news reporting originating from Washington, D.C., by NewsRx journalists, a trademark application has been made for "REIMAGINING RENEWABLES" by Carla L. Gannon Dilworth & Barrese, LLP, representing Create Energy, LLC. This application was made available to the public on April 19, 2026.


The serial number for this application is 98748950.
The international trademark goods and services class code for this trademark application is 009.
As submitted by the applicant, this trademark application relates to the following goods and services: Electrical components for renewable energy systems, namely, transformers and switchgears.
The registrar information for this application is: Carla L. GannonDilworth & Barrese, LLP, 48 South Service Road, Suite 200, Melville, NY 11747, United States.
Keywords for this news article include: Business, Create Energy LLC.
Our reports deliver fact-based news of research and discoveries from around the world. Copyright 2026, NewsRx LLC

urn:contentItem:6JK7-89S3-S2RD-H3R4-00000-00India bans sugar exports to protect supply2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisNEWSIndia bans sugar exports to protect supply

NEW DELHI: India, the world’s second-largest sugar producer, has banned exports until end-September, according to an official notice, as the government seeks to protect local supplies. The move follows...

NEW DELHI: India, the world’s second-largest sugar producer, has banned exports until end-September, according to an official notice, as the government seeks to protect local supplies. The move follows a cut in output estimates from a major millers’ group....

NEW DELHI: India, the world’s second-largest sugar producer, has banned exports until end-September, according to an official notice, as the government seeks to protect local supplies. The move follows a cut in output estimates from a major millers’ group. India’s gross sugar production is forecast at 32 million tonnes in the season ending Sept 30, down from an earlier projection of 32.4 million tonnes, the Indian Sugar & Bio-Energy Manufacturers Association said. The country’s next harvest, which starts around October, could be also affected by a lower-than-average monsoon. The annual rains starting next month are expected to be curbed by the El Nino weather pattern. The country is also a major fertiliser importer and is grappling with a spike in global prices driven by the war in Iran. Powered by: Ad will close in:3 seconds orclick to close The government in April had ruled out curbing overseas sales. However, the notice reversed those plans, prohibiting exports aside from limited exceptions, including for ships already loading. The global market has been awash in supply, and benchmark sugar futures in New York reached a five-year low earlier this year. However, some analysts have trimmed their outlooks for a surplus in the 2026 to 2027 season. Prices are now trading about 15% above those levels. The war in Iran has also stoked global demand for biofuels, which some countries make from sugar. India’s sugar exports have been a major swing factor in the global market in recent years. The country started limiting sugar exports following a poor crop in 2022 to 2023, issuing annual quotas for permitted sales. This season, the government permitted an initial 1.5 million tonnes for export in November and expanded that by 500,000 tonnes in February. — Bloomberg

value.Title: How First Solar Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-YV13-SHN4-41CV-00000-00How First Solar Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:07ZLexisNexisHow First Solar Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

During President Trump's second term, corporate investment in the United States has increased, with major companies in technology, manufacturing, and healthcare allocating billions to domestic expansion. The semiconductor industry has committed over $500 billion in private-sector funding, indicating a notable reshoring trend. This investment activity is backed by enhanced tax incentives from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, including 100% bonus depreciation and 35% manufacturing credits, and tariff policies aimed at supporting domestic production.

The reshoring trend affects multiple high-value sectors. Semiconductor manufacturing investments of over $500 billion across U.S. facilities are expected to create more than 150,000 new jobs, while pharmaceutical companies have allocated over $150 billion for U.S. manufacturing investments. Foreign direct investment was recorded at $102 billion in Q2 2025, representing a 137% increase from Q1. This comprehensive restructuring is influenced by both policy incentives and supply chain resilience priorities.

This report examines investment commitments from companies across the technology, manufacturing, healthcare, and energy sectors.

First Solar Inc. (NASDAQ: FSLR)

- Company Profile: First Solar Inc., a U.S.-headquartered manufacturer of solar panels, maintains production facilities worldwide and employs about 6,400 people.

- Announced Investment: An allocation of $1.1 billion in 2025 will fund the construction of a new solar panel manufacturing plant in Ohio. A separate $1.1 billion, 3.5 GW facility in Louisiana is scheduled to be operational in late 2025.

- Recipient: First Solar U.S. Solar Panel Plants

- Industries Impacted: Renewable Energy, Manufacturing

Baker Hughes Company (NASDAQ: BKR)

- Company Profile: Baker Hughes Company (NASDAQ: BKR) is a U.S.-based energy technology firm that supplies oilfield services and equipment, with about 55,000 employees globally.

- Announced Investment: $1.5 billion is allocated for 2025-2026 to advance carbon capture and hydrogen technology, and expand U.S. manufacturing for energy transition equipment.

- Recipient: Baker Hughes U.S. Energy Transition Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Energy Technology, Manufacturing

Cheniere Energy, Inc. (NYSE: LNG)

- Company Profile: Cheniere Energy, Inc. (NYSE: LNG) is a U.S.-based LNG firm that runs liquefaction and export facilities, employing approximately 1,800 people globally.

- Announced Investment: A $4 billion investment for 2025-2026 is aimed at expanding the Sabine Pass and Corpus Christi LNG facilities to increase export capacity.

- Recipient: Cheniere Energy U.S. LNG Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Energy, LNG Export

Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED)

- Company Profile: Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED) is a U.S.-based utility that provides electric, gas, and steam services, with a global employee count of approximately 15,000.

- Announced Investment: $2.8 billion planned for 2025-2026 to upgrade the New York City electric grid, improve resilience to weather, and increase renewable energy connections.

- Recipient: Consolidated Edison U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

Crown Castle International (NYSE: CCI)

- Company Profile: Crown Castle International (NYSE: CCI) is a U.S.-based telecommunications infrastructure firm that manages towers and fiber networks, with a team of approximately 7,000.

- Announced Investment: $3.5 billion for 2025-2026 to build out fiber-to-the-premises networks and 5G infrastructure throughout the U.S.

- Recipient: Crown Castle U.S. Telecom Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Telecommunications, Infrastructure.

Deere and Company

- Company Profile: Deere and Company (NYSE: DE) is a firm specializing in agricultural machinery and equipment manufacturing, employing approximately 82,000 people worldwide.

- Announced Investment: $2 billion in 2025 to enlarge Illinois and Iowa manufacturing facilities for the production of advanced machinery.

- Recipient: Deere U.S. Manufacturing Plants.

- Industries Impacted: Manufacturing, Agriculture.

Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: FANG)

- Company Profile: Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: FANG) is a U.S.-based oil and gas firm concentrated on Permian Basin operations, employing approximately 900 people globally.

- Announced Investment: $3.8 billion in a 2025 capital program for drilling and completion activities in the Permian Basin, increasing production capacity.

- Recipient: Diamondback Energy U.S. Permian Operations.

- Industries Impacted: Energy, Oil and Gas.

DTE Energy Company (NYSE: DTE)

- Company Profile: DTE Energy Company (NYSE: DTE) is a U.S.-based utility firm that supplies electric and gas services, employing approximately 10,000 people worldwide.

- Announced Investment: $2.3 billion for 2025-2026 to increase renewable energy capacity, update the Michigan electric grid, and develop natural gas infrastructure.

- Recipient: DTE Energy U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ: EXC)

- Company Profile: Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ: EXC) is a U.S.-based utility company that operates electric and gas utilities, with a global workforce of approximately 33,000.

- Announced Investment: $4.5 billion for 2025-2026 to modernize electric grid infrastructure, improve reliability, and increase clean energy integration in its service territories.

- Recipient: Exelon U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy.

FirstEnergy Corp. (NYSE: FE)

- Company Profile: FirstEnergy Corp. is an energy infrastructure operator with a workforce of approximately 15,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A multi-year investment of $15 billion is designated for infrastructure upgrades and grid modernization projects.

- Recipient: FirstEnergy U.S. Energy Grid

- Industries Impacted: Energy

Occidental Petroleum (NYSE: OXY)

- Company Profile: A U.S. energy company with a workforce of approximately 12,000 employees.

- Announced Investment: A $1.8 billion investment is planned for July 2025 for Texas enhanced oil recovery and direct air capture (DAC) plants.

- Recipient: U.S. energy sites

- Industries Impacted: Energy

ONEOK Inc. (NYSE: OKE)

- Company Profile: ONEOK, Inc., a U.S.-based energy company, manages extensive natural gas pipeline networks and employs approximately 3,000 individuals.

- Announced Investment: In August 2025, ONEOK confirmed its involvement in the Eiger Express Pipeline, a $1.2 billion, An error occurred during generation.

- Industries Impacted: Energy

Announced Investment: In August 2025, ONEOK confirmed its involvement in the Eiger Express Pipeline, a $1.2 billion, An error occurred during generation.Industries Impacted: Energy

Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE: PEG)

- Company Profile: Public Service Enterprise Group is a U.S.-based energy firm managing utilities and power generation, employing approximately 13,000 people globally.

- Announced Investment: A sum of $3.2 billion is designated for 2025-2026 to build offshore wind infrastructure, update transmission systems, and increase clean energy generation in New Jersey.

- Recipient: Public Service Enterprise Group U.S. Energy Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Renewable Energy

Rollins Inc. (NYSE: ROL)

- Company Profile: Rollins Inc. (NYSE: ROL) is a U.S.-headquartered pest control and termite services firm, with a global workforce of around 18,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A $1 billion capital injection in 2025 is allocated for fleet modernization and facility expansions.

- Recipient: Rollins U.S. Operations Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Services, Facilities

Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT)

- Company Profile: Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT), a prominent U.S.-headquartered retailer, manages stores and distribution networks with a workforce of around 440,000 employees.

- Announced Investment: A $1 billion development plan for 2025 is intended to improve distribution centers in Minnesota to accommodate e-commerce expansion.

- Recipient: Target U.S. Retail Operations

- Industries Impacted: Retail, Logistics

Teradyne Inc. (NASDAQ: TER)

- Company Profile: Teradyne Inc. (NASDAQ: TER) is a U.S.-headquartered semiconductor test equipment producer, with a global workforce of around 5,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A $1.4 billion capital injection in 2025 is set for the expansion of manufacturing and R&D facilities.

- Recipient: Teradyne U.S. Manufacturing Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Semiconductors, Manufacturing

Xcel Energy Inc. (NASDAQ: XEL)

- Company Profile: Xcel Energy Inc. is a U.S.-based utility company that delivers electricity and natural gas and has a global workforce of approximately 11,000.

- Announced Investment: A $2.5 billion expenditure in 2025-2026 will support renewable energy generation, transmission upgrades, and distribution modernization across its service area.

- Recipient: Xcel Energy U.S. Utility Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Renewable Energy.

Zoom Video Communications Inc. (NASDAQ: ZM)

- Company Profile: Zoom Video Communications Inc. is a U.S.-based video conferencing and communications platform company with a global staff of approximately 9,000.

- Announced Investment: A $1.5 billion fund in 2025 is planned for the expansion of data center infrastructure and AI capabilities.

- Recipient: Zoom U.S. Technology Infrastructure.

- Industries Impacted: Technology, Cloud Computing.

A year ago the First Solar, Inc. share price was $127.16. $1,000 would have bought 7.9 First Solar, Inc. shares on that day. Those 7.9 Shares would be worth $1,725 at today's share price of $219.31. PV$1000= $1,725.

Activities

First Solar, Inc., a leading American solar technology company listed on the Nasdaq, specializes in the design, manufacture, and sale of photovoltaic solar modules using advanced thin-film cadmium telluride (CdTe) technology. The company's core activities encompass the production of high-efficiency solar panels, as well as engineering, procurement, and construction services for large-scale solar projects, alongside operations and maintenance support. In recent years, First Solar's fastest-growing segments include utility-scale solar installations and international markets, driven by increasing global demand for renewable energy solutions, particularly in regions like Asia-Pacific and Europe. This expansion is fueled by technological innovations that enhance module efficiency and reduce costs, positioning the company as a key contributor to the transition toward sustainable energy. With a strong focus on environmental responsibility, First Solar continues to scale its operations while maintaining competitive advantages in cost and performance.

First Solar panels are at the forefront of photovoltaic technology, showcasing impressive First Solar module efficiency and First Solar module performance. Known for their innovative First Solar thin film solar panels, the company has made significant strides with First Solar CdTe modules and advances such as the First Solar Series 7 modules and First Solar Series 6 modules. Catering to large-scale energy needs, First Solar utility-scale modules are reinforced by First Solar domestic manufacturing, with FSLR manufacturing in US prominently positioned in their First Solar factory Ohio. This domestic focus is pivotal to the FSLR investment narrative domestic manufacturing and ensures First Solar supply chain resilience.

By prioritizing First Solar solar module recycling, the company promotes sustainability alongside First Solar sustainable manufacturing practices. With substantial First Solar production capacity and renowned First Solar PV modules for utility, investors regard their First Solar bankable modules as premium. First Solar’s robust First Solar module warranty and project-centric First Solar project development modules underscore their market leadership. The First Solar thin film technology advantages yield competitive First Solar cost per watt and minimal First Solar module degradation rate, distinguishing First Solar OEM modules. Renowned for First Solar module testing standards, their First Solar renewable energy modules contribute meaningfully to employment with First Solar manufacturing jobs US, supporting a strong FSLR stock domestic manufacturing edge while maintaining low First Solar lifecycle emissions CdTe.

value.Title: Euclid Acquires Thresh to Expand AI-Powered Diligence Capabilities in Renewable Energy Software

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-9B03-RYDK-D2KF-00000-00Euclid Acquires Thresh to Expand AI-Powered Diligence Capabilities in Renewable Energy Software2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisEuclid Acquires Thresh to Expand AI-Powered Diligence Capabilities in Renewable Energy Software

MANAVIGATOR-May 6, 2026-Euclid Acquires Thresh to Expand AI-Powered Diligence Capabilities in Renewable Energy Software


New York-based renewable energy M&A operating system Euclid, providing software and advisory services for project development, financing, and operations, has acquired US-based ai platform for infrastructure and renewable energy investment diligence Thresh Power, the company said.

Terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

Euclid will integrate Thresh's AI capabilities into its suite of software and services, expanding the tools its renewable energy specialists can use to deliver services across the project lifecycle, from transaction management and financing to compliance and construction oversight.

Euclid is backed by investors including Venrock, HSBC Asset Management, and Spero. Thresh was backed by Informed Ventures and Siam Capital.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED) is a leading energy utility company headquartered in New York, serving over 3.5 million customers in New York City and Westchester County. Its core business activities encompass the transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity, natural gas, and steam, supporting residential, commercial, and industrial sectors with a focus on reliability and safety. The company operates through subsidiaries like Consolidated Edison Company of New York and Orange and Rockland Utilities, emphasizing infrastructure maintenance and regulatory compliance. Fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, as well as electric vehicle infrastructure and grid modernization initiatives, driven by rising demand for sustainable energy solutions and state-level clean energy mandates. These efforts have bolstered ConEd's revenue growth, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon economy while maintaining operational excellence. November 06 2025: CON EDISON REPORTS 2025 THIRD QUARTER EARNINGS

Consolidated Edison, Inc. (Con Edison) (NYSE: ED) today reported 2025 third quarter net income for common stock of $688 million or $1.91 a share compared with $588 million or $1.70 a share in the 2024 third quarter. Adjusted earnings (non-GAAP) were $686 million or $1.90 a share in the 2025 period compared with $583 million or $1.68 a share in the 2024 period. Adjusted earnings and adjusted earnings per share in the 2025 and 2024 periods exclude accretion of the basis difference of Con Edison's equity investment in Mountain Valley Pipeline, LLC (MVP). Adjusted earnings and adjusted earnings per share in the 2024 period exclude the effects of hypothetical liquidation at book value (HLBV) accounting for tax equity investments.

For the first nine months of 2025, net income for common stock was $1,726 million or $4.84 a share compared with $1,510 million or $4.37 a share in the first nine months of 2024. Adjusted earnings were $1,718 million or $4.82 a share in the 2025 period compared with $1,528 million or $4.42 a share in the 2024 period. Adjusted earnings and adjusted earnings per share in the 2025 and 2024 periods exclude accretion of the basis difference of Con Edison's equity investment in MVP and adjustments to the gain and other impacts related to the sale of all of the stock of its former subsidiary, the Clean Energy Businesses, in 2023. Adjusted earnings and adjusted earnings per share in the 2024 period exclude the effects of HLBV accounting for tax equity investments.

"We continue to deliver the safe, resilient grid New Yorkers rely on and have reached a Joint Settlement Agreement on a three-year investment plan that, if approved, will fund critical infrastructure investments while keeping affordability and reliability front and center. 3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 89 words

Historic Rate Case Settlement Drastically Reduces Customer Bill Increases

The New York State Public Service Commission approved a three-year rate settlement for Consolidated Edison, significantly reducing proposed bill increases. Annual electric rate hikes were cut from 13.4% to 2.8%, and gas rates from 19% to 2%. The agreement, supported by multiple stakeholders, also includes provisions for improved language access, expanded low-income program outreach, and enhanced GHG accounting. Financially, the settlement provides revenue predictability for Con Edison's $38 billion capital plan through 2028, reducing regulatory risk for investors while addressing customer affordability, particularly for those transitioning to heat pumps.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 84 words

Q3 2025 Earnings Beat Consensus; 51-Year Dividend Aristocrat Continues Growth

Consolidated Edison reported strong Q3 2025 financial results, with adjusted earnings per share of $1.90, exceeding analyst expectations of $1.76. This performance was driven by increases in the company's regulated rate base and new rate plans. Con Edison subsequently raised its full-year earnings guidance and declared its quarterly dividend, continuing a 51-year streak of increases. The results were bolstered by $2.35 billion in recent infrastructure upgrades, reinforcing for investors the utility's reliable earnings power and its position as a defensive investment with predictable growth.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 86 words

$38 Billion Capital Investment Plan Approved; Targets 8.2% Annual Rate Base Growth Through 2029

Consolidated Edison has received regulatory approval for a $38 billion capital investment plan for 2025-2029, aimed at modernizing the grid, enhancing reliability, and supporting New York's clean energy goals. The plan is projected to drive an 8.2% compound annual growth rate in the company's rate base, providing long-term financial visibility. A transparent equity issuance plan is in place to fund the investments. For investors, this approval confirms regulatory support for the company's strategic infrastructure projects, which underpin its financial stability and capacity for sustainable dividend growth.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 89 words

NYISO Issues Critical Grid Reliability Warning; NYC Faces Dangerously Thin Reliability Margins Starting 2026

The New York Independent System Operator (NYISO) has warned that New York City will face a high risk of service disruptions starting in summer 2026 due to dangerously thin reliability margins. The issue stems from rising electricity demand, generator retirements, and transmission limits. NYISO identified several key projects, including the Champlain Hudson Power Express and Con Edison's Reliable Clean City projects, as essential to preventing reliability violations. This warning validates the strategic need for Con Edison's infrastructure investments and strengthens its position for rate recovery on these critical projects.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 87 words

Reliable Clean City Transmission Projects Add 900 MW Capacity Across NYC Boroughs

Consolidated Edison has advanced its Reliable Clean City program, an $800 million initiative that has added 900 MW of new transmission capacity across Queens, Brooklyn, and Staten Island. These projects are crucial for integrating renewable energy and enabling the retirement of older fossil fuel plants, supporting New York's 2040 zero-emission electricity goal. The on-schedule, on-budget execution of these projects, identified by NYISO as critical for grid reliability, enhances investor confidence in Con Edison's ability to manage its larger capital plan and supports its projected rate base growth.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 84 words

Brooklyn Clean Energy Hub Advances Offshore Wind Integration; 6 GW Capacity Target Established

Consolidated Edison is developing the Brooklyn Clean Energy Hub, an $810 million project designed to integrate up to 6,000 megawatts (6 GW) of offshore wind power into NYC's grid. The hub will create a new substation to address transmission bottlenecks, facilitating New York's goal of procuring 9,000 MW of offshore wind by 2035. The project has received regulatory approval and is designed with resilience features to serve disadvantaged communities. For investors, it represents a long-term, revenue-generating asset with strong regulatory backing and predictable returns.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 85 words

Champlain Hudson Power Express on Track for May 2026 In-Service; 1,250 MW Clean Hydropower Import Facility Nears Completion

The Champlain Hudson Power Express (CHPE), a $4.5 billion transmission line, is on schedule for its May 2026 in-service date. The project will transmit 1,250 MW of clean hydropower from Quebec to New York City, addressing the city's impending grid reliability issues. Identified by NYISO as a "permanent solution" to near-term needs, CHPE is expected to reduce electricity costs and CO2 emissions. For Con Edison, the project's completion is integral to its broader strategy of integrating large-scale renewable energy, reducing risk and boosting investor confidence.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Record $2.35 Billion Summer Infrastructure Investment Enhances Reliability and Electrification Readiness

Consolidated Edison has invested $2.35 billion in electric delivery system upgrades across its service area to prepare for rising summer demand and long-term electrification growth. The investments included new transformers, cables, and substation equipment, strategically targeting areas with high projected demand. These upgrades, executed while maintaining service reliability, support the company's projected 8.2% rate base growth and provide the regulatory basis for rate recovery, demonstrating to investors the company's capacity for effective capital deployment.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 77 words

Consolidated Edison Company of New York Issues $900 Million Long-Term Debt; $500 Million Term Loan Refinances Near-Term Obligations

In November 2025, Con Edison's subsidiary, CECONY, raised $1.4 billion in debt to fund its capital program. This included $900 million in 30-year debentures at a 5.75% coupon and a $500 million short-term loan to refinance existing obligations. The successful financing demonstrates strong market confidence in CECONY's credit quality and regulated business model. This disciplined, staggered approach to financing provides capital structure flexibility while securing long-term funding for infrastructure investments projected to drive significant rate base growth.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 79 words

Energy Affordability Program Delivers Record $311 Million in Low-Income Customer Discounts; 408,000-466,000 Customers Served

In 2024, Con Edison provided a record $311 million in bill discounts to over 408,000 low-income customers through its Energy Affordability Program (EAP), a 17% increase from 2023. The program, which aims to cap energy bills at 6% of household income, is expanding in 2026 to include more moderate-income households. This initiative demonstrates the company's commitment to customer affordability amid its large-scale infrastructure investments, enhancing its regulatory relationships and bolstering investor confidence in the stability of its operating environment.

Apr 22, 2026: Anterix Closes Benton PUD 900 MHz Spectrum Deal, Extending Utility Private-LTE Ecosystem Relevant to Consolidated Edison's Urban Grid Modernization Plans

On April 21, 2026, Anterix Inc. disclosed that its subsidiary PDV Spectrum Holding Company, LLC, entered into a spectrum license sale agreement with Public Utility District No. 1 of Benton County for a 10 MHz 900 MHz broadband license in Washington. According to industry reports, the agreement, valued at $771,000, supports a utility-owned network spanning 927 square miles and serving more than 58,000 customers.

Market data indicates the transaction represents the first private wireless broadband deployment among publicly owned utilities in the Pacific Northwest. The deployment supports grid automation, field connectivity, and outage response.

Impact on Consolidated Edison: ED

Following this development, Consolidated Edison gains another peer-utility reference deployment relevant to communications infrastructure planning across its New York City and Westchester County service territories. Operational metrics indicate Con Edison serves approximately 3.6 million electric, 1.1 million gas, and 1,500 steam customers in one of the most complex and densely populated urban utility environments in the U.S.

Analysis shows the Benton PUD agreement extends the national base of deployed utility 900 MHz private LTE networks, maturing the vendor ecosystem and operational playbooks relevant to any private wireless investment Con Edison might evaluate as part of its grid hardening and electrification programs aligned with New York's Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED) is a leading energy utility company headquartered in New York, serving over 3.5 million customers in New York City and Westchester County. Its core business activities encompass the transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity, natural gas, and steam, supporting residential, commercial, and industrial sectors with a focus on reliability and safety. The company operates through subsidiaries like Consolidated Edison Company of New York and Orange and Rockland Utilities, emphasizing infrastructure maintenance and regulatory compliance. Fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, as well as electric vehicle infrastructure and grid modernization initiatives, driven by rising demand for sustainable energy solutions and state-level clean energy mandates. These efforts have bolstered ConEd's revenue growth, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon economy while maintaining operational excellence. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

January 15: Con Edison to Report 2025 Earnings on February 19

/PRNewswire/ Consolidated Edison, Inc. (Con Edison) (NYSE:ED) plans to report its 2025 earnings on February 19, 2026 after the market closes.

Consolidated Edison, Inc. is a holding company that provides a wide range of energy-related products and services to its customers through the following subsidiaries: Consolidated Edison Company of New York, Inc., a regulated utility providing electric service in New York City and New York's Westchester County, gas service in Manhattan, the Bronx, parts of Queens and parts of Westchester, and steam service in Manhattan; Orange and Rockland Utilities, Inc., a regulated utility serving customers in a 1,300-square-mile area in southeastern New York State and northern New Jersey; and Con Edison Transmission, Inc., a regulated company primarily under the oversight of the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission, that develops and invests in electric transmission projects and owns, through joint ventures, both electric and gas assets.

November 06 2025: CON EDISON REPORTS 2025 THIRD QUARTER EARNINGS

NEW YORK, Nov. 6, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Consolidated Edison, Inc. (Con Edison) (NYSE: ED) today reported 2025 third quarter net income for common stock of $688 million or $1.91 a share compared with $588 million or $1.70 a share in the 2024 third quarter. Adjusted earnings (non-GAAP) were $686 million or $1.90 a share in the 2025 period compared with $583 million or $1.68 a share in the 2024 period. Adjusted earnings and adjusted earnings per share in the 2025 and 2024 periods exclude accretion of the basis difference of Con Edison's equity investment in Mountain Valley Pipeline, LLC (MVP). Adjusted earnings and adjusted earnings per share in the 2024 period exclude the effects of hypothetical liquidation at book value (HLBV) accounting for tax equity investments.

For the first nine months of 2025, net income for common stock was $1,726 million or $4.84 a share compared with $1,510 million or $4.37 a share in the first nine months of 2024. Adjusted earnings were $1,718 million or $4.82 a share in the 2025 period compared with $1,528 million or $4.42 a share in the 2024 period. Adjusted earnings and adjusted earnings per share in the 2025 and 2024 periods exclude accretion of the basis difference of Con Edison's equity investment in MVP and adjustments to the gain and other impacts related to the sale of all of the stock of its former subsidiary, the Clean Energy Businesses, in 2023. Adjusted earnings and adjusted earnings per share in the 2024 period exclude the effects of HLBV accounting for tax equity investments.

(Con Edison) (NYSE: ED) today reported 2025 third quarter net income for common stock of $688 million or $1.91 a share compared with $588 million or $1.70 a share in the 2024 third quarter.
